{{Museum | Name = Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall | Image = [[File:Takayama festival floats exhibition hall, Takayama, 2015.jpg|300px]] | Image Description = | Formal Name = | Nickname = | Predecessor = | Specialization = {{ill|Takayama Festival|ja|高山祭}} | Collection Size = | Visitors = | Director = | Curators = | Research Staff = | Operating Body = {{ill|Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|ja|桜山八幡宮}} | Management = | Annual Budget = | Building Design = | Site Area = | Building Area = | Total Floor Area = | Opened = {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43年}} | Closed = | Location Postal Code = 506-0858 | Location = {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}, {{ill|Takayama|ja|高山市}}, Sakura-machi 178
On the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine | Latitude Degrees = 36 | Latitude Minutes = 8 | Latitude Seconds = 54.0 | N (North) or S (South) = N | Longitude Degrees = 137 | Longitude Minutes = 15 | Longitude Seconds = 36.5 | E (East) or W (West) = E | Map Country Code = JP-21 | Show Coordinates at Top = | Access = | URL = }} '''Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall''' (高山祭屋台会館, Takayama Matsuri Yatai Kaikan) is a {{ill|museum|ja|博物館}} located in {{ill|Takayama|ja|高山市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}. == Overview == The museum houses and exhibits the {{ill|floats|ja|山車}} from the {{ill|Autumn Takayama Festival|ja|高山祭#秋の八幡祭}}, a festival celebrated as part of the {{ill|Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|ja|桜山八幡宮}}'s {{ill|annual festivals|ja|例祭}}. The festival has been designated as a {{ill|National Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要有形民俗文化財}} and {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} and is registered as a {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|無形文化遺産}}. The museum opened in {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43年}}{{Cite web|url=http://www.hidahachimangu.jp/hachiman/yuisho.html|title=Shrine History|publisher=Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|access-date=2022-03-21}}. It is located within the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine, which also manages the museum. The museum includes an annex, the {{ill|Sakurayama Nikko Hall|ja|桜山日光館}}. The museum is designated by {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}} as an equivalent to a museum (designated facility).{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/400187.pdf|title=Registered Museums and Designated Facilities in Gifu Prefecture|format=PDF|publisher=Gifu Prefecture|access-date=2024-06-30}} It is also registered under Gifu Prefecture's {{ill|Machikado Museums|ja|岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館}} program. == Main Exhibits == * Floats: Four out of the eleven floats from the Autumn Takayama Festival are displayed, with three changes annually. * Mikoshi (portable shrine) * Training Room * Treasures == Sakurayama Nikko Hall == * Opened in {{ill|1995|ja|平成7年}}. * Displays a 1/10 scale model of the {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮}}, created over 15 years during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代}} by a team of 33 artisans led by Hasegawa Kijūrō.The lead craftsman, originally from Toyama Prefecture. == Visitor Information == * Address: Sakura-machi 178, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture (on the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine) * Hours: 9:00 AM–5:00 PM (March–November), 9:00 AM–4:30 PM (December–February){{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/256201.pdf|title=Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall & Sakurayama Nikko Hall|format=PDF|publisher=Gifu Prefecture|access-date=2022-03-21}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-gifu.jp/spot/detail_1244.html|title=Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine & Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall|publisher=Gifu Travel Guide|access-date=2022-03-21}} * Closed: Open year-round * Admission: Adults ¥1,000, High school students ¥600, Elementary/Junior high school students ¥500 (includes entry to both Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall and Sakurayama Nikko Hall) * Parking: 50 spaces (paid) == Access == === Public Transportation === * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Takayama Station|ja|高山駅}} on the {{ill|JR Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of museums in Japan|ja|日本の博物館の一覧}} * {{ill|Floats|ja|山車}} * {{ill|Float Exhibition {{Museum | Name = Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall | Image = [[File:Takayama festival floats exhibition hall, Takayama, 2015.jpg|300px]] | Image Description = | Formal Name = | Nickname = | Predecessor = | Specialization = {{ill|Takayama Festival|ja|高山祭}} | Collection Size = | Visitors = | Director = | Curators = | Research Staff = | Operating Body = {{ill|Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|ja|桜山八幡宮}} | Management = | Annual Budget = | Building Design = | Site Area = | Building Area = | Total Floor Area = | Opened = {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43年}} | Closed = | Location Postal Code = 506-0858 | Location = {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}, {{ill|Takayama|ja|高山市}}, Sakura-machi 178
On the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine | Latitude Degrees = 36 | Latitude Minutes = 8 | Latitude Seconds = 54.0 | N (North) or S (South) = N | Longitude Degrees = 137 | Longitude Minutes = 15 | Longitude Seconds = 36.5 | E (East) or W (West) = E | Map Country Code = JP-21 | Show Coordinates at Top = | Access = | URL = }} '''Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall''' (高山祭屋台会館, Takayama Matsuri Yatai Kaikan) is a {{ill|museum|ja|博物館}} located in {{ill|Takayama|ja|高山市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}. == Overview == The museum houses and exhibits the {{ill|floats|ja|山車}} from the {{ill|Autumn Takayama Festival|ja|高山祭#秋の八幡祭}}, a festival celebrated as part of the {{ill|Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|ja|桜山八幡宮}}'s {{ill|annual festivals|ja|例祭}}. The festival has been designated as a {{ill|National Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要有形民俗文化財}} and {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} and is registered as a {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|無形文化遺産}}. The museum opened in {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43年}}{{Cite web|url=http://www.hidahachimangu.jp/hachiman/yuisho.html|title=Shrine History|publisher=Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|access-date=2022-03-21}}. It is located within the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine, which also manages the museum. The museum includes an annex, the {{ill|Sakurayama Nikko Hall|ja|桜山日光館}}. The museum is designated by {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}} as an equivalent to a museum (designated facility).{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/400187.pdf|title=Registered Museums and Designated Facilities in Gifu Prefecture|format=PDF|publisher=Gifu Prefecture|access-date=2024-06-30}} It is also registered under Gifu Prefecture's {{ill|Machikado Museums|ja|岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館}} program. == Main Exhibits == * Floats: Four out of the eleven floats from the Autumn Takayama Festival are displayed, with three changes annually. * Mikoshi (portable shrine) * Training Room * Treasures == Sakurayama Nikko Hall == * Opened in {{ill|1995|ja|平成7年}}. * Displays a 1/10 scale model of the {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮}}, created over 15 years during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代}} by a team of 33 artisans led by Hasegawa Kijūrō.The lead craftsman, originally from Toyama Prefecture. == Visitor Information == * Address: Sakura-machi 178, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture (on the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine) * Hours: 9:00 AM–5:00 PM (March–November), 9:00 AM–4:30 PM (December–February){{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/256201.pdf|title=Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall & Sakurayama Nikko Hall|format=PDF|publisher=Gifu Prefecture|access-date=2022-03-21}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-gifu.jp/spot/detail_1244.html|title=Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine & Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall|publisher=Gifu Travel Guide|access-date=2022-03-21}} * Closed: Open year-round * Admission: Adults ¥1,000, High school students ¥600, Elementary/Junior high school students ¥500 (includes entry to both Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall and Sakurayama Nikko Hall) * Parking: 50 spaces (paid) == Access == === Public Transportation === * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Takayama Station|ja|高山駅}} on the {{ill|JR Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of museums in Japan|ja|日本の博物館の一覧}} * {{ill|Floats|ja|山車}} * {{ill|Float Exhibition {{Museum | Name = Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall | Image = [[File:Takayama festival floats exhibition hall, Takayama, 2015.jpg|300px]] | Image Description = | Formal Name = | Nickname = | Predecessor = | Specialization = {{ill|Takayama Festival|ja|高山祭}} | Collection Size = | Visitors = | Director = | Curators = | Research Staff = | Operating Body = {{ill|Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|ja|桜山八幡宮}} | Management = | Annual Budget = | Building Design = | Site Area = | Building Area = | Total Floor Area = | Opened = {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43年}} | Closed = | Location Postal Code = 506-0858 | Location = {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}, {{ill|Takayama|ja|高山市}}, Sakura-machi 178
On the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine | Latitude Degrees = 36 | Latitude Minutes = 8 | Latitude Seconds = 54.0 | N (North) or S (South) = N | Longitude Degrees = 137 | Longitude Minutes = 15 | Longitude Seconds = 36.5 | E (East) or W (West) = E | Map Country Code = JP-21 | Show Coordinates at Top = | Access = | URL = }} '''Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall''' (高山祭屋台会館, Takayama Matsuri Yatai Kaikan) is a {{ill|museum|ja|博物館}} located in {{ill|Takayama|ja|高山市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}. == Overview == The museum houses and exhibits the {{ill|floats|ja|山車}} from the {{ill|Autumn Takayama Festival|ja|高山祭#秋の八幡祭}}, a festival celebrated as part of the {{ill|Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|ja|桜山八幡宮}}'s {{ill|annual festivals|ja|例祭}}. The festival has been designated as a {{ill|National Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要有形民俗文化財}} and {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} and is registered as a {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|無形文化遺産}}. The museum opened in {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43年}}{{Cite web|url=http://www.hidahachimangu.jp/hachiman/yuisho.html|title=Shrine History|publisher=Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|access-date=2022-03-21}}. It is located within the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine, which also manages the museum. The museum includes an annex, the {{ill|Sakurayama Nikko Hall|ja|桜山日光館}}. The museum is designated by {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}} as an equivalent to a museum (designated facility).{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/400187.pdf|title=Registered Museums and Designated Facilities in Gifu Prefecture|format=PDF|publisher=Gifu Prefecture|access-date=2024-06-30}} It is also registered under Gifu Prefecture's {{ill|Machikado Museums|ja|岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館}} program. == Main Exhibits == * Floats: Four out of the eleven floats from the Autumn Takayama Festival are displayed, with three changes annually. * Mikoshi (portable shrine) * Training Room * Treasures == Sakurayama Nikko Hall == * Opened in {{ill|1995|ja|平成7年}}. * Displays a 1/10 scale model of the {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮}}, created over 15 years during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代}} by a team of 33 artisans led by Hasegawa Kijūrō.The lead craftsman, originally from Toyama Prefecture. == Visitor Information == * Address: Sakura-machi 178, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture (on the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine) * Hours: 9:00 AM–5:00 PM (March–November), 9:00 AM–4:30 PM (December–February){{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/256201.pdf|title=Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall & Sakurayama Nikko Hall|format=PDF|publisher=Gifu Prefecture|access-date=2022-03-21}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-gifu.jp/spot/detail_1244.html|title=Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine & Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall|publisher=Gifu Travel Guide|access-date=2022-03-21}} * Closed: Open year-round * Admission: Adults ¥1,000, High school students ¥600, Elementary/Junior high school students ¥500 (includes entry to both Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall and Sakurayama Nikko Hall) * Parking: 50 spaces (paid) == Access == === Public Transportation === * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Takayama Station|ja|高山駅}} on the {{ill|JR Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of museums in Japan|ja|日本の博物館の一覧}} * {{ill|Floats|ja|山車}} * {{ill|Float Exhibition {{Museum | Name = Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall | image = [[File:Takayama festival floats exhibition hall, Takayama, 2015.jpg|300px]] | image Description = | Formal Name = | Nickname = | Predecessor = | Specialization = {{ill|Takayama Festival|ja|高山祭}} | Collection Size = | Visitors = | Director = | Curators = | Research Staff = | Operating Body = {{ill|Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|ja|桜山八幡宮}} | Management = | Annual Budget = | Building Design = | Site Area = | Building Area = | Total Floor Area = | Opened = {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43年}} | Closed = | Location Postal Code = 506-0858 | Location = {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}, {{ill|Takayama|ja|高山市}}, Sakura-machi 178
On the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine | Latitude Degrees = 36 | Latitude Minutes = 8 | Latitude Seconds = 54.0 | N (North) or S (South) = N | Longitude Degrees = 137 | Longitude Minutes = 15 | Longitude Seconds = 36.5 | E (East) or W (West) = E | Map Country Code = JP-21 | Show Coordinates at Top = | Access = | URL = }} '''Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall''' (高山祭屋台会館, Takayama Matsuri Yatai Kaikan) is a {{ill|museum|ja|博物館}} located in {{ill|Takayama|ja|高山市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}. == Overview == The museum houses and exhibits the {{ill|floats|ja|山車}} from the {{ill|Autumn Takayama Festival|ja|高山祭#秋の八幡祭}}, a festival celebrated as part of the {{ill|Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|ja|桜山八幡宮}}'s {{ill|annual festivals|ja|例祭}}. The festival has been designated as a {{ill|National Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要有形民俗文化財}} and {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} and is registered as a {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|無形文化遺産}}. The museum opened in {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43年}}{{Cite web|url=http://www.hidahachimangu.jp/hachiman/yuisho.html|title=Shrine History|publisher=Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|access-date=2022-03-21}}. It is located within the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine, which also manages the museum. The museum includes an annex, the {{ill|Sakurayama Nikko Hall|ja|桜山日光館}}. The museum is designated by {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}} as an equivalent to a museum (designated facility).{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/400187.pdf|title=Registered Museums and Designated Facilities in Gifu Prefecture|format=PDF|publisher=Gifu Prefecture|access-date=2024-06-30}} It is also registered under Gifu Prefecture's {{ill|Machikado Museums|ja|岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館}} program. == Main Exhibits == * Floats: Four out of the eleven floats from the Autumn Takayama Festival are displayed, with three changes annually. * Mikoshi (portable shrine) * Training Room * Treasures == Sakurayama Nikko Hall == * Opened in {{ill|1995|ja|平成7年}}. * Displays a 1/10 scale model of the {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮}}, created over 15 years during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代}} by a team of 33 artisans led by Hasegawa Kijūrō.The lead craftsman, originally from Toyama Prefecture. == Visitor Information == * Address: Sakura-machi 178, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture (on the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine) * Hours: 9:00 AM–5:00 PM (March–November), 9:00 AM–4:30 PM (December–February){{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/256201.pdf|title=Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall & Sakurayama Nikko Hall|format=PDF|publisher=Gifu Prefecture|access-date=2022-03-21}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-gifu.jp/spot/detail_1244.html|title=Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine & Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall|publisher=Gifu Travel Guide|access-date=2022-03-21}} * Closed: Open year-round * Admission: Adults ¥1,000, High school students ¥600, Elementary/Junior high school students ¥500 (includes entry to both Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall and Sakurayama Nikko Hall) * Parking: 50 spaces (paid) == Access == === Public Transportation === * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Takayama Station|ja|高山駅}} on the {{ill|JR Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of museums in Japan|ja|日本の博物館の一覧}} * {{ill|Floats|ja|山車}} * {{ill|Float Exhibition {{Museum | Name = Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall | image = [[File:Takayama festival floats exhibition hall, Takayama, 2015.jpg|300px]] | image Description = | Formal Name = | Nickname = | Predecessor = | Specialization = {{ill|Takayama Festival|ja|高山祭}} | Collection Size = | Visitors = | Director = | Curators = | Research Staff = | Operating Body = {{ill|Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|ja|桜山八幡宮}} | Management = | Annual Budget = | Building Design = | Site Area = | Building Area = | Total Floor Area = | Opened = {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43年}} | Closed = | Location Postal Code = 506-0858 | Location = {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}, {{ill|Takayama|ja|高山市}}, Sakura-machi 178
On the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine | Latitude Degrees = 36 | Latitude Minutes = 8 | Latitude Seconds = 54.0 | N (North) or S (South) = N | Longitude Degrees = 137 | Longitude Minutes = 15 | Longitude Seconds = 36.5 | E (East) or W (West) = E | Map Country Code = JP-21 | Show Coordinates at Top = | Access = | URL = }} '''Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall''' (高山祭屋台会館, Takayama Matsuri Yatai Kaikan) is a {{ill|museum|ja|博物館}} located in {{ill|Takayama|ja|高山市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}. == Overview == The museum houses and exhibits the {{ill|floats|ja|山車}} from the {{ill|Autumn Takayama Festival|ja|高山祭#秋の八幡祭}}, a festival celebrated as part of the {{ill|Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|ja|桜山八幡宮}}'s {{ill|annual festivals|ja|例祭}}. The festival has been designated as a {{ill|National Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要有形民俗文化財}} and {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} and is registered as a {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|無形文化遺産}}. The museum opened in {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43年}}{{Cite web|url=http://www.hidahachimangu.jp/hachiman/yuisho.html|title=Shrine History|publisher=Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|access-date=2022-03-21}}. It is located within the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine, which also manages the museum. The museum includes an annex, the {{ill|Sakurayama Nikko Hall|ja|桜山日光館}}. The museum is designated by {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}} as an equivalent to a museum (designated facility).{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/400187.pdf|title=Registered Museums and Designated Facilities in Gifu Prefecture|format=PDF|publisher=Gifu Prefecture|access-date=2024-06-30}} It is also registered under Gifu Prefecture's {{ill|Machikado Museums|ja|岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館}} program. == Main Exhibits == * Floats: Four out of the eleven floats from the Autumn Takayama Festival are displayed, with three changes annually. * Mikoshi (portable shrine) * Training Room * Treasures == Sakurayama Nikko Hall == * Opened in {{ill|1995|ja|平成7年}}. * Displays a 1/10 scale model of the {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮}}, created over 15 years during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代}} by a team of 33 artisans led by Hasegawa Kijūrō.The lead craftsman, originally from Toyama Prefecture. == Visitor Information == * Address: Sakura-machi 178, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture (on the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine) * Hours: 9:00 AM–5:00 PM (March–November), 9:00 AM–4:30 PM (December–February){{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/256201.pdf|title=Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall & Sakurayama Nikko Hall|format=PDF|publisher=Gifu Prefecture|access-date=2022-03-21}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-gifu.jp/spot/detail_1244.html|title=Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine & Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall|publisher=Gifu Travel Guide|access-date=2022-03-21}} * Closed: Open year-round * Admission: Adults ¥1,000, High school students ¥600, Elementary/Junior high school students ¥500 (includes entry to both Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall and Sakurayama Nikko Hall) * Parking: 50 spaces (paid) == Access == === Public Transportation === * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Takayama Station|ja|高山駅}} on the {{ill|JR Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of museums in Japan|ja|日本の博物館の一覧}} * {{ill|Floats|ja|山車}} * {{ill|Float Exhibition Halls|ja|山車会館}} == External Links == * [http://www.hidahachimangu.jp/yataikaikan/index.html Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall] - Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine * {{Museum | Name = Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall | image = [[File:Takayama festival floats exhibition hall, Takayama, 2015.jpg|300px]] | image Description = | Formal Name = | Nickname = | Predecessor = | Specialization = {{ill|Takayama Festival|ja|高山祭}} | Collection Size = | Visitors = | Director = | Curators = | Research Staff = | Operating Body = {{ill|Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|ja|桜山八幡宮}} | Management = | Annual Budget = | Building Design = | Site Area = | Building Area = | Total Floor Area = | Opened = {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43rd year }} | Closed = | Location Postal Code = 506-0858 | Location = {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}, {{ill|Takayama|ja|高山市}}, Sakura-machi 178
On the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine | Latitude Degrees = 36 | Latitude Minutes = 8 | Latitude Seconds = 54.0 | N (North) or S (South) = N | Longitude Degrees = 137 | Longitude Minutes = 15 | Longitude Seconds = 36.5 | E (East) or W (West) = E | Map Country Code = JP-21 | Show Coordinates at Top = | Access = | URL = }} '''Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall''' (高山祭屋台会館, Takayama Matsuri Yatai Kaikan) is a {{ill|museum|ja|博物館}} located in {{ill|Takayama|ja|高山市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}. == Overview == The museum houses and exhibits the {{ill|floats|ja|山車}} from the {{ill|Autumn Takayama Festival|ja|高山祭#秋の八幡祭}}, a festival celebrated as part of the {{ill|Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|ja|桜山八幡宮}}'s {{ill|annual festivals|ja|例祭}}. The festival has been designated as a {{ill|National Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要有形民俗文化財}} and {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} and is registered as a {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|無形文化遺産}}. The museum opened in {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43rd year }}{{Cite web|url=http://www.hidahachimangu.jp/hachiman/yuisho.html|title=Shrine History|publisher=Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|access-date=2022-03-21}}. It is located within the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine, which also manages the museum. The museum includes an annex, the {{ill|Sakurayama Nikko Hall|ja|桜山日光館}}. The museum is designated by {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}} as an equivalent to a museum (designated facility).{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/400187.pdf|title=Registered Museums and Designated Facilities in Gifu Prefecture|format=PDF|publisher=Gifu Prefecture|access-date=2024-06-30}} It is also registered under Gifu Prefecture's {{ill|Machikado Museums|ja|岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館}} program. == Main Exhibits == * Floats: Four out of the eleven floats from the Autumn Takayama Festival are displayed, with three changes annually. * Mikoshi (portable shrine) * Training Room * Treasures == Sakurayama Nikko Hall == * Opened in {{ill|1995|ja|平成7年}}. * Displays a 1/10 scale model of the {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮}}, created over 15 years during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代}} by a team of 33 artisans led by Hasegawa Kijūrō.The lead craftsman, originally from Toyama Prefecture. == Visitor Information == * Address: Sakura-machi 178, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture (on the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine) * Hours: 9:00 AM–5:00 PM (March–November), 9:00 AM–4:30 PM (December–February){{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/256201.pdf|title=Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall & Sakurayama Nikko Hall|format=PDF|publisher=Gifu Prefecture|access-date=2022-03-21}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-gifu.jp/spot/detail_1244.html|title=Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine & Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall|publisher=Gifu Travel Guide|access-date=2022-03-21}} * Closed: Open year-round * Admission: Adults ¥1,000, High school students ¥600, Elementary/Junior high school students ¥500 (includes entry to both Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall and Sakurayama Nikko Hall) * Parking: 50 spaces (paid) == Access == === Public Transportation === * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Takayama Station|ja|高山駅}} on the {{ill|JR Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of museums in Japan|ja|日本の博物館の一覧}} * {{ill|Floats|ja|山車}} * {{ill|Float Exhibition Halls|ja|山車会館}} == External Links == * [http://www.hidahachimangu.jp/yataikaikan/index.html Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall] - Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine * [https://www.kankou-gifu.jp/spot/detail_1244.html Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine & Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall] - Gifu Travel Guide [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[Category:Completion tier {{Museum | Name = Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall | image = [[File:Takayama festival floats exhibition hall, Takayama, 2015.jpg|300px]] | image Description = | Formal Name = | Nickname = | Predecessor = | Specialization = {{ill|Takayama Festival|ja|高山祭}} | Collection Size = | Visitors = | Director = | Curators = | Research Staff = | Operating Body = {{ill|Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|ja|桜山八幡宮}} | Management = | Annual Budget = | Building Design = | Site Area = | Building Area = | Total Floor Area = | Opened = {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43rd year }} | Closed = | Location Postal Code = 506-0858 | Location = {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}, {{ill|Takayama|ja|高山市}}, Sakura-machi 178
On the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine | Latitude Degrees = 36 | Latitude Minutes = 8 | Latitude Seconds = 54.0 | N (North) or S (South) = N | Longitude Degrees = 137 | Longitude Minutes = 15 | Longitude Seconds = 36.5 | E (East) or W (West) = E | Map Country Code = JP-21 | Show Coordinates at Top = | Access = | URL = }} '''Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall''' (高山祭屋台会館, Takayama Matsuri Yatai Kaikan) is a {{ill|museum|ja|博物館}} located in {{ill|Takayama|ja|高山市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}. == Overview == The museum houses and exhibits the {{ill|floats|ja|山車}} from the {{ill|Autumn Takayama Festival|ja|高山祭#秋の八幡祭}}, a festival celebrated as part of the {{ill|Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|ja|桜山八幡宮}}'s {{ill|annual festivals|ja|例祭}}. The festival has been designated as a {{ill|National Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要有形民俗文化財}} and {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} and is registered as a {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|無形文化遺産}}. The museum opened in {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43rd year }}{{Cite web|url=http://www.hidahachimangu.jp/hachiman/yuisho.html|title=Shrine History|publisher=Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|access-date=2022-03-21}}. It is located within the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine, which also manages the museum. The museum includes an annex, the {{ill|Sakurayama Nikko Hall|ja|桜山日光館}}. The museum is designated by {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}} as an equivalent to a museum (designated facility).{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/400187.pdf|title=Registered Museums and Designated Facilities in Gifu Prefecture|format=PDF|publisher=Gifu Prefecture|access-date=2024-06-30}} It is also registered under Gifu Prefecture's {{ill|Machikado Museums|ja|岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館}} program. == Main Exhibits == * Floats: Four out of the eleven floats from the Autumn Takayama Festival are displayed, with three changes annually. * Mikoshi (portable shrine) * Training Room * Treasures == Sakurayama Nikko Hall == * Opened in {{ill|1995|ja|平成7th year }}. * Displays a 1/10 scale model of the {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮}}, created over 15 years during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代}} by a team of 33 artisans led by Hasegawa Kijūrō.The lead craftsman, originally from Toyama Prefecture. == Visitor Information == * Address: Sakura-machi 178, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture (on the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine) * Hours: 9:00 AM–5:00 PM (March–November), 9:00 AM–4:30 PM (December–February){{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/256201.pdf|title=Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall & Sakurayama Nikko Hall|format=PDF|publisher=Gifu Prefecture|access-date=2022-03-21}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-gifu.jp/spot/detail_1244.html|title=Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine & Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall|publisher=Gifu Travel Guide|access-date=2022-03-21}} * Closed: Open year-round * Admission: Adults ¥1,000, High school students ¥600, Elementary/Junior high school students ¥500 (includes entry to both Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall and Sakurayama Nikko Hall) * Parking: 50 spaces (paid) == Access == === Public Transportation === * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Takayama Station|ja|高山駅}} on the {{ill|JR Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of museums in Japan|ja|日本の博物館の一覧}} * {{ill|Floats|ja|山車}} * {{ill|Float Exhibition Halls|ja|山車会館}} == External Links == * [http://www.hidahachimangu.jp/yataikaikan/index.html Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall] - Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine * [https://www.kankou-gifu.jp/spot/detail_1244.html Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine & Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall] - Gifu Travel Guide [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Completion tier Halls|ja|山車会館}} == External Links == * [http://www.hidahachimangu.jp/yataikaikan/index.html Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall] - Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine * {{Museum | Name = Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall | image = [[File:Takayama festival floats exhibition hall, Takayama, 2015.jpg|300px]] | image Description = | Formal Name = | Nickname = | Predecessor = | Specialization = {{ill|Takayama Festival|ja|高山祭|12=draft}} | Collection Size = | Visitors = | Director = | Curators = | Research Staff = | Operating Body = {{ill|Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|ja|桜山八幡宮|12=draft}} | Management = | Annual Budget = | Building Design = | Site Area = | Building Area = | Total Floor Area = | Opened = {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43rd year|12=draft}} | Closed = | Location Postal Code = 506-0858 | Location = {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Takayama|ja|高山市|12=draft}}, Sakura-machi 178
On the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine | Latitude Degrees = 36 | Latitude Minutes = 8 | Latitude Seconds = 54.0 | N (North) or S (South) = N | Longitude Degrees = 137 | Longitude Minutes = 15 | Longitude Seconds = 36.5 | E (East) or W (West) = E | Map Country Code = JP-21 | Show Coordinates at Top = | Access = | URL = }} '''Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall''' (高山祭屋台会館, Takayama Matsuri Yatai Kaikan) is a {{ill|museum|ja|博物館|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Takayama|ja|高山市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県|12=draft}}. == Overview == The museum houses and exhibits the {{ill|floats|ja|山車|12=draft}} from the {{ill|Autumn Takayama Festival|ja|高山祭#秋の八幡祭|12=draft}}, a festival celebrated as part of the {{ill|Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|ja|桜山八幡宮|12=draft}}'s {{ill|annual festivals|ja|例祭|12=draft}}. The festival has been designated as a {{ill|National Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要有形民俗文化財|12=draft}} and {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=draft}} and is registered as a {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|無形文化遺産|12=draft}}. The museum opened in {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43rd year|12=draft}}{{Cite web|url=http://www.hidahachimangu.jp/hachiman/yuisho.html|title=Shrine History|publisher=Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|access-date=2022-03-21}}. It is located within the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine, which also manages the museum. The museum includes an annex, the {{ill|Sakurayama Nikko Hall|ja|桜山日光館|12=draft}}. The museum is designated by {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県|12=draft}} as an equivalent to a museum (designated facility).{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/400187.pdf|title=Registered Museums and Designated Facilities in Gifu Prefecture|format=PDF|publisher=Gifu Prefecture|access-date=2024-06-30}} It is also registered under Gifu Prefecture's {{ill|Machikado Museums|ja|岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館|12=draft}} program. == Main Exhibits == * Floats: Four out of the eleven floats from the Autumn Takayama Festival are displayed, with three changes annually. * Mikoshi (portable shrine) * Training Room * Treasures == Sakurayama Nikko Hall == * Opened in {{ill|1995|ja|平成7th year|12=draft}}. * Displays a 1/10 scale model of the {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮|12=draft}}, created over 15 years during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代|12=draft}} by a team of 33 artisans led by Hasegawa Kijūrō.The lead craftsman, originally from Toyama Prefecture. == Visitor Information == * Address: Sakura-machi 178, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture (on the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine) * Hours: 9:00 AM–5:00 PM (March–November), 9:00 AM–4:30 PM (December–February){{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/256201.pdf|title=Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall & Sakurayama Nikko Hall|format=PDF|publisher=Gifu Prefecture|access-date=2022-03-21}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-gifu.jp/spot/detail_1244.html|title=Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine & Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall|publisher=Gifu Travel Guide|access-date=2022-03-21}} * Closed: Open year-round * Admission: Adults ¥1,000, High school students ¥600, Elementary/Junior high school students ¥500 (includes entry to both Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall and Sakurayama Nikko Hall) * Parking: 50 spaces (paid) == Access == === Public Transportation === * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Takayama Station|ja|高山駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|JR Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線|12=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of museums in Japan|ja|日本の博物館の一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Floats|ja|山車|12=draft}} * {{ill|Float Exhibition Halls|ja|山車会館|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.hidahachimangu.jp/yataikaikan/index.html Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall] - Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine * [https://www.kankou-gifu.jp/spot/detail_1244.html Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine & Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall] - Gifu Travel Guide {{draft categories| {{Museum | Name = Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall | image = [[File:Takayama festival floats exhibition hall, Takayama, 2015.jpg|300px]] | image Description = | Formal Name = | Nickname = | Predecessor = | Specialization = {{ill|Takayama Festival|ja|高山祭|12=draft}} | Collection Size = | Visitors = | Director = | Curators = | Research Staff = | Operating Body = {{ill|Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|ja|桜山八幡宮|12=draft}} | Management = | Annual Budget = | Building Design = | Site Area = | Building Area = | Total Floor Area = | Opened = {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43rd year|12=draft}} | Closed = | Location Postal Code = 506-0858 | Location = {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Takayama|ja|高山市|12=draft}}, Sakura-machi 178
On the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine | Latitude Degrees = 36 | Latitude Minutes = 8 | Latitude Seconds = 54.0 | N (North) or S (South) = N | Longitude Degrees = 137 | Longitude Minutes = 15 | Longitude Seconds = 36.5 | E (East) or W (West) = E | Map Country Code = JP-21 | Show Coordinates at Top = | Access = | URL = }} '''Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall''' (高山祭屋台会館, Takayama Matsuri Yatai Kaikan) is a {{ill|museum|ja|博物館|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Takayama|ja|高山市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県|12=draft}}. == Overview == The museum houses and exhibits the {{ill|floats|ja|山車|12=draft}} from the {{ill|Autumn Takayama Festival|ja|高山祭#秋の八幡祭|12=draft}}, a festival celebrated as part of the {{ill|Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|ja|桜山八幡宮|12=draft}}'s {{ill|annual festivals|ja|例祭|12=draft}}. The festival has been designated as a {{ill|National Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要有形民俗文化財|12=draft}} and {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=draft}} and is registered as a {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|無形文化遺産|12=draft}}. The museum opened in {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43rd year|12=draft}}{{Cite web|url=http://www.hidahachimangu.jp/hachiman/yuisho.html|title=Shrine History|publisher=Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|access-date=2022-03-21}}. It is located within the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine, which also manages the museum. The museum includes an annex, the {{ill|Sakurayama Nikko Hall|ja|桜山日光館|12=draft}}. The museum is designated by {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県|12=draft}} as an equivalent to a museum (designated facility).{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/400187.pdf|title=Registered Museums and Designated Facilities in Gifu Prefecture|format=PDF|publisher=Gifu Prefecture|access-date=2024-06-30}} It is also registered under Gifu Prefecture's {{ill|Machikado Museums|ja|岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館|12=draft}} program. == Main Exhibits == * Floats: Four out of the eleven floats from the Autumn Takayama Festival are displayed, with three changes annually. * Mikoshi (portable shrine) * Training Room * Treasures == Sakurayama Nikko Hall == * Opened in {{ill|1995|ja|平成7th year|12=draft}}. * Displays a 1/10 scale model of the {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮|12=draft}}, created over 15 years during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代|12=draft}} by a team of 33 artisans led by Hasegawa Kijūrō.The lead craftsman, originally from Toyama Prefecture. == Visitor Information == * Address: Sakura-machi 178, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture (on the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine) * Hours: 9:00 AM–5:00 PM (March–November), 9:00 AM–4:30 PM (December–February){{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/256201.pdf|title=Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall & Sakurayama Nikko Hall|format=PDF|publisher=Gifu Prefecture|access-date=2022-03-21}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-gifu.jp/spot/detail_1244.html|title=Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine & Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall|publisher=Gifu Travel Guide|access-date=2022-03-21}} * Closed: Open year-round * Admission: Adults ¥1,000, High school students ¥600, Elementary/Junior high school students ¥500 (includes entry to both Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall and Sakurayama Nikko Hall) * Parking: 50 spaces (paid) == Access == === Public Transportation === * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Takayama Station|ja|高山駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|JR Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線|12=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of museums in Japan|ja|日本の博物館の一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Floats|ja|山車|12=draft}} * {{ill|Float Exhibition Halls|ja|山車会館|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.hidahachimangu.jp/yataikaikan/index.html Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall] - Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine * [https://www.kankou-gifu.jp/spot/detail_1244.html Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine & Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall] - Gifu Travel Guide {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall]] [[Category:Folk museums in Japan]] [[Category:Museums in Takayama]] [[Category:Museums established in 1968]] [[Category:Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall]] [[ja:高山祭屋台会館]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan}}

{{Museum | Name = Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall | image = [[File:Takayama festival floats exhibition hall, Takayama, 2015.jpg|300px]] | image Description = | Formal Name = | Nickname = | Predecessor = | Specialization = {{ill|Takayama Festival|ja|高山祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Collection Size = | Visitors = | Director = | Curators = | Research Staff = | Operating Body = {{ill|Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|ja|桜山八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Management = | Annual Budget = | Building Design = | Site Area = | Building Area = | Total Floor Area = | Opened = {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Closed = | Location Postal Code = 506-0858 | Location = {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Takayama|ja|高山市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Sakura-machi 178
On the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine | Latitude Degrees = 36 | Latitude Minutes = 8 | Latitude Seconds = 54.0 | N (North) or S (South) = N | Longitude Degrees = 137 | Longitude Minutes = 15 | Longitude Seconds = 36.5 | E (East) or W (West) = E | Map Country Code = JP-21 | Show Coordinates at Top = | Access = | URL = }} '''Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall''' (高山祭屋台会館, Takayama Matsuri Yatai Kaikan) is a {{ill|museum|ja|博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Takayama|ja|高山市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Overview == The museum houses and exhibits the {{ill|floats|ja|山車|qq=draft|qq=draft}} from the {{ill|Autumn Takayama Festival|ja|高山祭#秋の八幡祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a festival celebrated as part of the {{ill|Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|ja|桜山八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s {{ill|annual festivals|ja|例祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The festival has been designated as a {{ill|National Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要有形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and is registered as a {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|無形文化遺産|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The museum opened in {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{Cite web|url=http://www.hidahachimangu.jp/hachiman/yuisho.html|title=Shrine History|publisher=Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|access-date=2022-03-21}}. It is located within the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine, which also manages the museum. The museum includes an annex, the {{ill|Sakurayama Nikko Hall|ja|桜山日光館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The museum is designated by {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as an equivalent to a museum (designated facility).{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/400187.pdf|title=Registered Museums and Designated Facilities in Gifu Prefecture|format=PDF|publisher=Gifu Prefecture|access-date=2024-06-30}} It is also registered under Gifu Prefecture's {{ill|Machikado Museums|ja|岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} program. == Main Exhibits == * Floats: Four out of the eleven floats from the Autumn Takayama Festival are displayed, with three changes annually. * Mikoshi (portable shrine) * Training Room * Treasures == Sakurayama Nikko Hall == * Opened in {{ill|1995|ja|平成7th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * Displays a 1/10 scale model of the {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, created over 15 years during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} by a team of 33 artisans led by Hasegawa Kijūrō.The lead craftsman, originally from Toyama Prefecture. == Visitor Information == * Address: Sakura-machi 178, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture (on the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine) * Hours: 9:00 AM–5:00 PM (March–November), 9:00 AM–4:30 PM (December–February){{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/256201.pdf|title=Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall & Sakurayama Nikko Hall|format=PDF|publisher=Gifu Prefecture|access-date=2022-03-21}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-gifu.jp/spot/detail_1244.html|title=Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine & Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall|publisher=Gifu Travel Guide|access-date=2022-03-21}} * Closed: Open year-round * Admission: Adults ¥1,000, High school students ¥600, Elementary/Junior high school students ¥500 (includes entry to both Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall and Sakurayama Nikko Hall) * Parking: 50 spaces (paid) == Access == === Public Transportation === * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Takayama Station|ja|高山駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|JR Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of museums in Japan|ja|日本の博物館の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Floats|ja|山車|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Float Exhibition Halls|ja|山車会館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.hidahachimangu.jp/yataikaikan/index.html Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall] - Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine * [https://www.kankou-gifu.jp/spot/detail_1244.html Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine & Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall] - Gifu Travel Guide {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall]] [[Category:Folk museums in Japan]] [[Category:Museums in Takayama]] [[Category:Museums established in 1968]] [[Category:Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall]] [[ja:高山祭屋台会館]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan}}

revision.comment: 8 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:高山祭屋台会館 up to rev 102411490
revision.text:
{{博物館 |名称 = 高山祭屋台会館 |画像 = [[File:Takayama festival floats exhibition hall, Takayama, 2015.jpg|300px]] |画像説明 = |正式名称 = |愛称 = |前身 = |専門分野 = [[高山祭]] |収蔵作品数 = |来館者数 = |館長 = |学芸員 = |研究職員 = |事業主体 = [[桜山八幡宮]] |管理運営 = |年運営費 = |建物設計 = |敷地面積 = |建築面積 = |延床面積 = |開館 = [[1968年]]（昭和43年） |閉館 = |所在地郵便番号= 506-0858 |所在地= [[岐阜県]][[高山市]]桜町178
桜山八幡宮境内 |緯度度 = 36 |緯度分 = 8 |緯度秒 = 54.0 |N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 137 |経度分 = 15 |経度秒 = 36.5 |E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP-21 |座標右上表示 = |アクセス = |URL = }} '''高山祭屋台会館'''（たかやままつりやたいかいかん）は、[[岐阜県]][[高山市]]にある[[博物館]]（[[山車会館]]）である。 == 概要 == [[桜山八幡宮]]の[[例祭]]であり、国の[[重要有形民俗文化財]]および[[重要無形民俗文化財]]に指定ならびに、[[ユネスコ]]の[[無形文化遺産]]に登録されている[[高山祭#秋の八幡祭|秋の高山祭]]の[[山車|屋台]]を保管展示する施設である。[[1968年]]（昭和43年）開館{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.hidahachimangu.jp/hachiman/yuisho.html|title=御由緒 |format= |publisher=桜山八幡宮 |accessdate=2022-03-21}}。[[桜山八幡宮]]境内にあり、桜山八幡宮が管理運営する。附設館として'''[[#桜山日光館|桜山日光館]]'''がある。 岐阜県より博物館に相当する施設（指定施設）として指定されている{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/400187.pdf |title=岐阜県内の登録博物館、指定施設一覧 |format=PDF |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2024-06-30}}。また、岐阜県独自の[[岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館]]に登録されている。 == 主な展示 == * 屋台 : 秋の高山祭の屋台全11台のうち4台が展示。毎年3回入替が行われる * 大神輿 * 研修室 * 宝物 == 桜山日光館 == * [[1995年]]（平成7年）7月開館。 * [[大正時代]]、長谷川喜十郎富山県出身の仏師。ら33人の技術者によって、15年間の歳月をかけて製作された10分の1スケールの[[日光東照宮]]模型を展示する。 == 利用案内 == * 住所：[[岐阜県]][[高山市]]桜町178（桜山八幡宮境内） * 開館時間：9:00 - 17:00（3月 - 11月）、9:00 - 16:30（12月 - 2月）{{Cite web|和書|url= https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/256201.pdf|title=高山祭屋台会館・桜山日光館 |format= PDF|publisher=岐阜県（まちかど美術館・博物館） |accessdate=2022-03-21}}{{Cite web|和書|url= https://www.kankou-gifu.jp/spot/detail_1244.html|title=桜山八幡宮・高山祭屋台会館 |format=|publisher=岐阜の旅ガイド |accessdate=2022-03-21}} * 休館日：無休 * 料金：大人1000円、高校生600円、小中学生500円　※高山祭屋台会館、桜山日光館共通 * 駐車場：普通車50台（有料） == 交通アクセス == === 公共交通機関 === * [[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[高山本線]][[高山駅]]から徒歩で約20分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[日本の博物館の一覧]] * [[山車]] * [[山車会館]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.hidahachimangu.jp/yataikaikan/index.html　高山祭屋台会館] - 桜山八幡宮 * [https://www.kankou-gifu.jp/spot/detail_1244.html　桜山八幡宮・高山祭屋台会館] - 岐阜の旅ガイド {{デフォルトソート:たかやままつりやたいかいかん}} [[Category:日本の民俗博物館]] [[Category:高山市の博物館]] [[Category:1968年開設の博物館]]

{{Museum | Name = Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall | image = [[File:Takayama festival floats exhibition hall, Takayama, 2015.jpg|300px]] | image Description = | Formal Name = | Nickname = | Predecessor = | Specialization = {{ill|Takayama Festival|ja|高山祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Collection Size = | Visitors = | Director = | Curators = | Research Staff = | Operating Body = {{ill|Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|ja|桜山八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Management = | Annual Budget = | Building Design = | Site Area = | Building Area = | Total Floor Area = | Opened = {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Closed = | Location Postal Code = 506-0858 | Location = {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Takayama|ja|高山市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Sakura-machi 178
On the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine | Latitude Degrees = 36 | Latitude Minutes = 8 | Latitude Seconds = 54.0 | N (North) or S (South) = N | Longitude Degrees = 137 | Longitude Minutes = 15 | Longitude Seconds = 36.5 | E (East) or W (West) = E | Map Country Code = JP-21 | Show Coordinates at Top = | Access = | URL = }} '''Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall''' (高山祭屋台会館, Takayama Matsuri Yatai Kaikan) is a {{ill|museum|ja|博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Takayama|ja|高山市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Overview == The museum houses and exhibits the {{ill|floats|ja|山車|qq=draft|qq=draft}} from the {{ill|Autumn Takayama Festival|ja|高山祭#秋の八幡祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a festival celebrated as part of the {{ill|Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|ja|桜山八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s {{ill|annual festivals|ja|例祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The festival has been designated as a {{ill|National Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要有形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and is registered as a {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|無形文化遺産|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The museum opened in {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{Cite web|url=http://www.hidahachimangu.jp/hachiman/yuisho.html|title=Shrine History|publisher=Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|access-date=2022-03-21}}. It is located within the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine, which also manages the museum. The museum includes an annex, the {{ill|Sakurayama Nikko Hall|ja|桜山日光館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The museum is designated by {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as an equivalent to a museum (designated facility).{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/400187.pdf|title=Registered Museums and Designated Facilities in Gifu Prefecture|format=PDF|publisher=Gifu Prefecture|access-date=2024-06-30}} It is also registered under Gifu Prefecture's {{ill|Machikado Museums|ja|岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} program. == Main Exhibits == * Floats: Four out of the eleven floats from the Autumn Takayama Festival are displayed, with three changes annually. * Mikoshi (portable shrine) * Training Room * Treasures == Sakurayama Nikko Hall == * Opened in {{ill|1995|ja|平成7th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * Displays a 1/10 scale model of the {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, created over 15 years during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} by a team of 33 artisans led by Hasegawa Kijūrō.The lead craftsman, originally from Toyama Prefecture. == Visitor Information == * Address: Sakura-machi 178, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture (on the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine) * Hours: 9:00 AM–5:00 PM (March–November), 9:00 AM–4:30 PM (December–February){{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/256201.pdf|title=Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall & Sakurayama Nikko Hall|format=PDF|publisher=Gifu Prefecture|access-date=2022-03-21}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-gifu.jp/spot/detail_1244.html|title=Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine & Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall|publisher=Gifu Travel Guide|access-date=2022-03-21}} * Closed: Open year-round * Admission: Adults ¥1,000, High school students ¥600, Elementary/Junior high school students ¥500 (includes entry to both Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall and Sakurayama Nikko Hall) * Parking: 50 spaces (paid) == Access == === Public Transportation === * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Takayama Station|ja|高山駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|JR Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of museums in Japan|ja|日本の博物館の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Floats|ja|山車|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Float Exhibition Halls|ja|山車会館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.hidahachimangu.jp/yataikaikan/index.html Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall] - Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine * [https://www.kankou-gifu.jp/spot/detail_1244.html Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine & Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall] - Gifu Travel Guide {{DEFAULTSORT:Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan}} {{translated page|ja|高山祭屋台会館|version=102411490|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall]] [[Category:Folk museums in Japan]] [[Category:Museums in Takayama]] [[Category:Museums established in 1968]] [[Category:Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall]] [[ja:高山祭屋台会館]]

{{Museum | Name = Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall | image = [[File:Takayama festival floats exhibition hall, Takayama, 2015.jpg|300px]] | image Description = | Formal Name = | Nickname = | Predecessor = | Specialization = {{ill|Takayama Festival|ja|高山祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takayama Festival|qq=}} | Collection Size = | Visitors = | Director = | Curators = | Research Staff = | Operating Body = {{ill|Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|ja|桜山八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} | Management = | Annual Budget = | Building Design = | Site Area = | Building Area = | Total Floor Area = | Opened = {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1968|qq=}} | Closed = | Location Postal Code = 506-0858 | Location = {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gifu Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Takayama|ja|高山市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takayama|qq=}}, Sakura-machi 178
On the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine | Latitude Degrees = 36 | Latitude Minutes = 8 | Latitude Seconds = 54.0 | N (North) or S (South) = N | Longitude Degrees = 137 | Longitude Minutes = 15 | Longitude Seconds = 36.5 | E (East) or W (West) = E | Map Country Code = JP-21 | Show Coordinates at Top = | Access = | URL = }} '''Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall''' (高山祭屋台会館, Takayama Matsuri Yatai Kaikan) is a {{ill|museum|ja|博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/museum|qq=}} located in {{ill|Takayama|ja|高山市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takayama|qq=}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gifu Prefecture|qq=}}. == Overview == The museum houses and exhibits the {{ill|floats|ja|山車|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/floats|qq=}} from the {{ill|Autumn Takayama Festival|ja|高山祭#秋の八幡祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Takayama Festival|qq=}}, a festival celebrated as part of the {{ill|Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|ja|桜山八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|qq=}}'s {{ill|annual festivals|ja|例祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/annual festivals|qq=}}. The festival has been designated as a {{ill|National Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要有形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} and {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} and is registered as a {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|無形文化遺産|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|qq=}}. The museum opened in {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1968|qq=}}{{Cite web|url=http://www.hidahachimangu.jp/hachiman/yuisho.html|title=Shrine History|publisher=Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|access-date=2022-03-21}}. It is located within the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine, which also manages the museum. The museum includes an annex, the {{ill|Sakurayama Nikko Hall|ja|桜山日光館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurayama Nikko Hall|qq=}}. The museum is designated by {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gifu Prefecture|qq=}} as an equivalent to a museum (designated facility).{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/400187.pdf|title=Registered Museums and Designated Facilities in Gifu Prefecture|format=PDF|publisher=Gifu Prefecture|access-date=2024-06-30}} It is also registered under Gifu Prefecture's {{ill|Machikado Museums|ja|岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Machikado Museums|qq=}} program. == Main Exhibits == * Floats: Four out of the eleven floats from the Autumn Takayama Festival are displayed, with three changes annually. * Mikoshi (portable shrine) * Training Room * Treasures == Sakurayama Nikko Hall == * Opened in {{ill|1995|ja|平成7th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1995|qq=}}. * Displays a 1/10 scale model of the {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō Tōshō-gū|qq=}}, created over 15 years during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō period|qq=}} by a team of 33 artisans led by Hasegawa Kijūrō.The lead craftsman, originally from Toyama Prefecture. == Visitor Information == * Address: Sakura-machi 178, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture (on the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine) * Hours: 9:00 AM–5:00 PM (March–November), 9:00 AM–4:30 PM (December–February){{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/256201.pdf|title=Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall & Sakurayama Nikko Hall|format=PDF|publisher=Gifu Prefecture|access-date=2022-03-21}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-gifu.jp/spot/detail_1244.html|title=Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine & Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall|publisher=Gifu Travel Guide|access-date=2022-03-21}} * Closed: Open year-round * Admission: Adults ¥1,000, High school students ¥600, Elementary/Junior high school students ¥500 (includes entry to both Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall and Sakurayama Nikko Hall) * Parking: 50 spaces (paid) == Access == === Public Transportation === * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Takayama Station|ja|高山駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takayama Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|JR Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Takayama Main Line|qq=}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of museums in Japan|ja|日本の博物館の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of museums in Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|Floats|ja|山車|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Floats|qq=}} * {{ill|Float Exhibition Halls|ja|山車会館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Float Exhibition Halls|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.hidahachimangu.jp/yataikaikan/index.html Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall] - Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine * [https://www.kankou-gifu.jp/spot/detail_1244.html Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine & Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall] - Gifu Travel Guide {{translated page|ja|高山祭屋台会館|version=102411490|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall]] [[Category:Folk museums in Japan]] [[Category:Museums in Takayama]] [[Category:Museums established in 1968]] [[Category:Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan]] }} [[sn:Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall]] [[ja:高山祭屋台会館]]

On the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine | Latitude Degrees = 36 | Latitude Minutes = 8 | Latitude Seconds = 54.0 | N (North) or S (South) = N | Longitude Degrees = 137 | Longitude Minutes = 15 | Longitude Seconds = 36.5 | E (East) or W (West) = E | Map Country Code = JP-21 | Show Coordinates at Top = | Access = | URL = }} '''Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall''' (高山祭屋台会館, Takayama Matsuri Yatai Kaikan) is a {{ill|museum|ja|博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/museum|qq=}} located in {{ill|Takayama|ja|高山市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takayama|qq=}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gifu Prefecture|qq=}}. == Overview == The museum houses and exhibits the {{ill|Matsuri float|lt=Matsuri float|lt=Floats|ja|山車|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/floats|qq=}} from the {{ill|Autumn Takayama Festival|ja|高山祭#秋の八幡祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Takayama Festival|qq=}}, a festival celebrated as part of the {{ill|Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|ja|桜山八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|qq=}}'s {{ill|annual festivals|ja|例祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/annual festivals|qq=}}. The festival has been designated as a {{ill|National Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要有形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} and {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} and is registered as a {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|無形文化遺産|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|qq=}}. The museum opened in {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1968|qq=}}{{Cite web|url=http://www.hidahachimangu.jp/hachiman/yuisho.html|title=Shrine History|publisher=Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|access-date=2022-03-21}}. It is located within the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine, which also manages the museum. The museum includes an annex, the {{ill|Sakurayama Nikko Hall|ja|桜山日光館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurayama Nikko Hall|qq=}}. The museum is designated by {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gifu Prefecture|qq=}} as an equivalent to a museum (designated facility).{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/400187.pdf|title=Registered Museums and Designated Facilities in Gifu Prefecture|format=PDF|publisher=Gifu Prefecture|access-date=2024-06-30}} It is also registered under Gifu Prefecture's {{ill|Machikado Museums|ja|岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Machikado Museums|qq=}} program. == Main Exhibits == * Floats: Four out of the eleven floats from the Autumn Takayama Festival are displayed, with three changes annually. * Mikoshi (portable shrine) * Training Room * Treasures == Sakurayama Nikko Hall == * Opened in {{ill|1995|ja|平成7th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1995|qq=}}. * Displays a 1/10 scale model of the {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō Tōshō-gū|qq=}}, created over 15 years during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō period|qq=}} by a team of 33 artisans led by Hasegawa Kijūrō.The lead craftsman, originally from Toyama Prefecture. == Visitor Information == * Address: Sakura-machi 178, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture (on the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine) * Hours: 9:00 AM–5:00 PM (March–November), 9:00 AM–4:30 PM (December–February){{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/256201.pdf|title=Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall & Sakurayama Nikko Hall|format=PDF|publisher=Gifu Prefecture|access-date=2022-03-21}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-gifu.jp/spot/detail_1244.html|title=Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine & Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall|publisher=Gifu Travel Guide|access-date=2022-03-21}} * Closed: Open year-round * Admission: Adults ¥1,000, High school students ¥600, Elementary/Junior high school students ¥500 (includes entry to both Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall and Sakurayama Nikko Hall) * Parking: 50 spaces (paid) == Access == === Public Transportation === * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Takayama Station|ja|高山駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takayama Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|JR Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Takayama Main Line|qq=}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of museums in Japan|ja|日本の博物館の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of museums in Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|Matsuri float|lt=Matsuri float|lt=Floats|ja|山車|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Floats|qq=}} * {{ill|Float Exhibition Halls|ja|山車会館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Float Exhibition Halls|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.hidahachimangu.jp/yataikaikan/index.html Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall] - Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine * [https://www.kankou-gifu.jp/spot/detail_1244.html Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine & Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall] - Gifu Travel Guide {{translated page|ja|高山祭屋台会館|version=102411490|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Folk museums in Japan]] [[Category:Museums in Takayama]] [[Category:Museums established in 1968]] }} [[sn:Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall]] [[ja:高山祭屋台会館]]

{{Museum | Name = Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall | image = [[File:Takayama festival floats exhibition hall, Takayama, 2015.jpg|300px]] | image Description = | Formal Name = | Nickname = | Predecessor = | Specialization = {{ill|Takayama Festival|ja|高山祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takayama Festival|qq=}} | Collection Size = | Visitors = | Director = | Curators = | Research Staff = | Operating Body = {{ill|Sakurayama Hachimangu|lt=Sakurayama Hachimangu|lt=Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|ja|桜山八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} | Management = | Annual Budget = | Building Design = | Site Area = | Building Area = | Total Floor Area = | Opened = {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1968|qq=}} | Closed = | Location Postal Code = 506-0858 | Location = {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gifu Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Takayama|ja|高山市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takayama|qq=}}, Sakura-machi 178
On the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine | Latitude Degrees = 36 | Latitude Minutes = 8 | Latitude Seconds = 54.0 | N (North) or S (South) = N | Longitude Degrees = 137 | Longitude Minutes = 15 | Longitude Seconds = 36.5 | E (East) or W (West) = E | Map Country Code = JP-21 | Show Coordinates at Top = | Access = | URL = }} '''Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall''' (高山祭屋台会館, Takayama Matsuri Yatai Kaikan) is a {{ill|museum|ja|博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/museum|qq=}} located in {{ill|Takayama|ja|高山市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takayama|qq=}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gifu Prefecture|qq=}}. == Overview == The museum houses and exhibits the {{ill|Matsuri float|lt=Matsuri float|lt=Floats|ja|山車|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/floats|qq=}} from the {{ill|Takayama Festival|lt=Takayama Festival|lt=Autumn Takayama Festival|ja|高山祭#秋の八幡祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Takayama Festival|qq=}}, a festival celebrated as part of the {{ill|Sakurayama Hachimangu|lt=Sakurayama Hachimangu|lt=Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|ja|桜山八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|qq=}}'s {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Annual festivals|ja|例祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/annual festivals|qq=}}. The festival has been designated as a {{ill|National Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要有形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} and {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} and is registered as a {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|無形文化遺産|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|qq=}}. The museum opened in {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1968|qq=}}{{Cite web|url=http://www.hidahachimangu.jp/hachiman/yuisho.html|title=Shrine History|publisher=Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|access-date=2022-03-21}}. It is located within the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine, which also manages the museum. The museum includes an annex, the {{ill|Sakurayama Nikko Hall|ja|桜山日光館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurayama Nikko Hall|qq=}}. The museum is designated by {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gifu Prefecture|qq=}} as an equivalent to a museum (designated facility).{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/400187.pdf|title=Registered Museums and Designated Facilities in Gifu Prefecture|format=PDF|publisher=Gifu Prefecture|access-date=2024-06-30}} It is also registered under Gifu Prefecture's {{ill|Machikado Museums|ja|岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Machikado Museums|qq=}} program. == Main Exhibits == * Floats: Four out of the eleven floats from the Autumn Takayama Festival are displayed, with three changes annually. * Mikoshi (portable shrine) * Training Room * Treasures == Sakurayama Nikko Hall == * Opened in {{ill|1995|ja|平成7th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1995|qq=}}. * Displays a 1/10 scale model of the {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō Tōshō-gū|qq=}}, created over 15 years during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō period|qq=}} by a team of 33 artisans led by Hasegawa Kijūrō.The lead craftsman, originally from Toyama Prefecture. == Visitor Information == * Address: Sakura-machi 178, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture (on the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine) * Hours: 9:00 AM–5:00 PM (March–November), 9:00 AM–4:30 PM (December–February){{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/256201.pdf|title=Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall & Sakurayama Nikko Hall|format=PDF|publisher=Gifu Prefecture|access-date=2022-03-21}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-gifu.jp/spot/detail_1244.html|title=Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine & Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall|publisher=Gifu Travel Guide|access-date=2022-03-21}} * Closed: Open year-round * Admission: Adults ¥1,000, High school students ¥600, Elementary/Junior high school students ¥500 (includes entry to both Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall and Sakurayama Nikko Hall) * Parking: 50 spaces (paid) == Access == === Public Transportation === * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Takayama Station|ja|高山駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takayama Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|JR Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Takayama Main Line|qq=}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of museums in Japan|ja|日本の博物館の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of museums in Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|Matsuri float|lt=Matsuri float|lt=Floats|ja|山車|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Floats|qq=}} * {{ill|Float Exhibition Halls|ja|山車会館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Float Exhibition Halls|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.hidahachimangu.jp/yataikaikan/index.html Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall] - Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine * [https://www.kankou-gifu.jp/spot/detail_1244.html Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine & Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall] - Gifu Travel Guide {{translated page|ja|高山祭屋台会館|version=102411490|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Folk museums in Japan]] [[Category:Museums in Takayama]] [[Category:Museums established in 1968]] }} [[sn:Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall]] [[ja:高山祭屋台会館]]

{{Museum | Name = Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall | image = [[File:Takayama festival floats exhibition hall, Takayama, 2015.jpg|300px]] | image Description = | Formal Name = | Nickname = | Predecessor = | Specialization = [[:en:Takayama Festival|Takayama Festival]] | Collection Size = | Visitors = | Director = | Curators = | Research Staff = | Operating Body = {{ill|Sakurayama Hachimangu|lt=Sakurayama Hachimangu|lt=Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|ja|桜山八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} | Management = | Annual Budget = | Building Design = | Site Area = | Building Area = | Total Floor Area = | Opened = {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1968|qq=}} | Closed = | Location Postal Code = 506-0858 | Location = [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]], [[:en:Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], Sakura-machi 178
On the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine | Latitude Degrees = 36 | Latitude Minutes = 8 | Latitude Seconds = 54.0 | N (North) or S (South) = N | Longitude Degrees = 137 | Longitude Minutes = 15 | Longitude Seconds = 36.5 | E (East) or W (West) = E | Map Country Code = JP-21 | Show Coordinates at Top = | Access = | URL = }} '''Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall''' (高山祭屋台会館, Takayama Matsuri Yatai Kaikan) is a [[:en:Museum|museum]] located in [[:en:Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]]. == Overview == The museum houses and exhibits the [[:en:Matsuri float|Floats]] from the {{ill|Takayama Festival|lt=Takayama Festival|lt=Autumn Takayama Festival|ja|高山祭#秋の八幡祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Takayama Festival|qq=}}, a festival celebrated as part of the {{ill|Sakurayama Hachimangu|lt=Sakurayama Hachimangu|lt=Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|ja|桜山八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|qq=}}'s {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Annual festivals|ja|例祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/annual festivals|qq=}}. The festival has been designated as a {{ill|National Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要有形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} and {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} and is registered as a [[:en:Intangible cultural heritage|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage]]. The museum opened in {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1968|qq=}}{{Cite web|url=http://www.hidahachimangu.jp/hachiman/yuisho.html|title=Shrine History|publisher=Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|access-date=2022-03-21}}. It is located within the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine, which also manages the museum. The museum includes an annex, the {{ill|Sakurayama Nikko Hall|ja|桜山日光館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurayama Nikko Hall|qq=}}. The museum is designated by [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]] as an equivalent to a museum (designated facility).{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/400187.pdf|title=Registered Museums and Designated Facilities in Gifu Prefecture|format=PDF|publisher=Gifu Prefecture|access-date=2024-06-30}} It is also registered under Gifu Prefecture's {{ill|Machikado Museums|ja|岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Machikado Museums|qq=}} program. == Main Exhibits == * Floats: Four out of the eleven floats from the Autumn Takayama Festival are displayed, with three changes annually. * Mikoshi (portable shrine) * Training Room * Treasures == Sakurayama Nikko Hall == * Opened in {{ill|1995|ja|平成7年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1995|qq=}}. * Displays a 1/10 scale model of the [[:en:Nikkō Tōshō-gū|Nikkō Tōshō-gū]], created over 15 years during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō period|qq=}} by a team of 33 artisans led by Hasegawa Kijūrō.The lead craftsman, originally from Toyama Prefecture. == Visitor Information == * Address: Sakura-machi 178, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture (on the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine) * Hours: 9:00 AM–5:00 PM (March–November), 9:00 AM–4:30 PM (December–February){{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/256201.pdf|title=Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall & Sakurayama Nikko Hall|format=PDF|publisher=Gifu Prefecture|access-date=2022-03-21}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-gifu.jp/spot/detail_1244.html|title=Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine & Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall|publisher=Gifu Travel Guide|access-date=2022-03-21}} * Closed: Open year-round * Admission: Adults ¥1,000, High school students ¥600, Elementary/Junior high school students ¥500 (includes entry to both Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall and Sakurayama Nikko Hall) * Parking: 50 spaces (paid) == Access == === Public Transportation === * Approximately a 20-minute walk from [[:en:Takayama Station|Takayama Station]] on the [[:en:Takayama Main Line|JR Takayama Main Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:List of museums in Japan|List of museums in Japan]] * [[:en:Matsuri float|Floats]] * {{ill|Float Exhibition Halls|ja|山車会館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Float Exhibition Halls|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.hidahachimangu.jp/yataikaikan/index.html Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall] - Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine * [https://www.kankou-gifu.jp/spot/detail_1244.html Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine & Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall] - Gifu Travel Guide {{translated page|ja|高山祭屋台会館|version=102411490|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Folk museums in Japan]] [[Category:Museums in Takayama]] [[Category:Museums established in 1968]] }} [[sn:Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall]] [[ja:高山祭屋台会館]]
