{{Museum | Name = Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall | image = [[File:Takayama festival floats exhibition hall, Takayama, 2015.jpg|300px]] | image Description = | Formal Name = | Nickname = | Predecessor = | Specialization = [[:en:Takayama Festival|Takayama Festival]] | Collection Size = | Visitors = | Director = | Curators = | Research Staff = | Operating Body = {{ill|Sakurayama Hachimangu|lt=Sakurayama Hachimangu|lt=Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|ja|桜山八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} | Management = | Annual Budget = | Building Design = | Site Area = | Building Area = | Total Floor Area = | Opened = {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1968|qq=}} | Closed = | Location Postal Code = 506-0858 | Location = [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]], [[:en:Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], Sakura-machi 178
On the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine | Latitude Degrees = 36 | Latitude Minutes = 8 | Latitude Seconds = 54.0 | N (North) or S (South) = N | Longitude Degrees = 137 | Longitude Minutes = 15 | Longitude Seconds = 36.5 | E (East) or W (West) = E | Map Country Code = JP-21 | Show Coordinates at Top = | Access = | URL = }} '''Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall''' (高山祭屋台会館, Takayama Matsuri Yatai Kaikan) is a [[:en:Museum|museum]] located in [[:en:Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]]. == Overview == The museum houses and exhibits the [[:en:Matsuri float|Floats]] from the {{ill|Takayama Festival|lt=Takayama Festival|lt=Autumn Takayama Festival|ja|高山祭#秋の八幡祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Takayama Festival|qq=}}, a festival celebrated as part of the {{ill|Sakurayama Hachimangu|lt=Sakurayama Hachimangu|lt=Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|ja|桜山八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|qq=}}'s {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Annual festivals|ja|例祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/annual festivals|qq=}}. The festival has been designated as a {{ill|National Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要有形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} and {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} and is registered as a [[:en:Intangible cultural heritage|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage]]. The museum opened in {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1968|qq=}}{{Cite web|url=http://www.hidahachimangu.jp/hachiman/yuisho.html|title=Shrine History|publisher=Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|access-date=2022-03-21}}. It is located within the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine, which also manages the museum. The museum includes an annex, the {{ill|Sakurayama Nikko Hall|ja|桜山日光館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurayama Nikko Hall|qq=}}. The museum is designated by [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]] as an equivalent to a museum (designated facility).{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/400187.pdf|title=Registered Museums and Designated Facilities in Gifu Prefecture|format=PDF|publisher=Gifu Prefecture|access-date=2024-06-30}} It is also registered under Gifu Prefecture's {{ill|Machikado Museums|ja|岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Machikado Museums|qq=}} program. == Main Exhibits == * Floats: Four out of the eleven floats from the Autumn Takayama Festival are displayed, with three changes annually. * Mikoshi (portable shrine) * Training Room * Treasures == Sakurayama Nikko Hall == * Opened in {{ill|1995|ja|平成7年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1995|qq=}}. * Displays a 1/10 scale model of the [[:en:Nikkō Tōshō-gū|Nikkō Tōshō-gū]], created over 15 years during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō period|qq=}} by a team of 33 artisans led by Hasegawa Kijūrō.The lead craftsman, originally from Toyama Prefecture. == The museum includes an annex, the {{ill|Sakurayama Nikko Hall|ja|桜山日光館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurayama Nikko Hall|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The museum is designated by [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]] as an equivalent to a museum (designated facility).{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/400187.pdf|title=Registered Museums and Designated Facilities in Gifu Prefecture|format=PDF|publisher=Gifu Prefecture|access-date=2024-06-30}} It is also registered under Gifu Prefecture's {{ill|Machikado Museums|ja|岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Machikado Museums|qq=}} program. == Main Exhibits == * Floats: Four out of the eleven floats from the Autumn Takayama Festival are displayed, with three changes annually. * Mikoshi (portable shrine) * Training Room * Treasures == Sakurayama Nikko Hall == * Opened in [[:en:1995|1995]]. * Displays a 1/10 scale model of the [[:en:Nikkō Tōshō-gū|Nikkō Tōshō-gū]], created over 15 years during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō period|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大正}} by a team of 33 artisans led by Hasegawa Kijūrō.The lead craftsman, originally from Toyama Prefecture. == Visitor Information == * Address: Sakura-machi 178, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture (on the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine) * Hours: 9:00 AM–5:00 PM (March–November), 9:00 AM–4:30 PM (December–February){{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/256201.pdf|title=Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall & Sakurayama Nikko Hall|format=PDF|publisher=Gifu Prefecture|access-date=2022-03-21}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-gifu.jp/spot/detail_1244.html|title=Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine & Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall|publisher=Gifu Travel Guide|access-date=2022-03-21}} * Closed: Open year-round * Admission: Adults ¥1,000, High school students ¥600, Elementary/Junior high school students ¥500 (includes entry to both Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall and Sakurayama Nikko Hall) * Parking: 50 spaces (paid) == Access == === Public Transportation === * Approximately a 20-minute walk from [[:en:Takayama Station|Takayama Station]] on the [[:en:Takayama Main Line|JR Takayama Main Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:List of museums in Japan|List of museums in Japan]] * [[:en:Matsuri float|Floats]] On the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine | Latitude Degrees = 36 | Latitude Minutes = 8 | Latitude Seconds = 54.0 | N (North) or S (South) = N | Longitude Degrees = 137 | Longitude Minutes = 15 | Longitude Seconds = 36.5 | E (East) or W (West) = E | Map Country Code = JP-21 | Show Coordinates at Top = | Access = | URL = }} '''Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall''' (高山祭屋台会館, Takayama Matsuri Yatai Kaikan) is a [[:en:Museum|museum]] located in [[:en:Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]]. == Overview == The museum houses and exhibits the [[:en:Matsuri float|Floats]] from the {{ill|Takayama Festival|lt=Takayama Festival|lt=Autumn Takayama Festival|ja|高山祭#秋の八幡祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Takayama Festival|qq=}}, a festival celebrated as part of the {{ill|Sakurayama Hachimangu|lt=Sakurayama Hachimangu|lt=Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|ja|桜山八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|qq=}}'s {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Annual festivals|ja|例祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/annual festivals|qq=}}. The festival has been designated as a {{ill|National Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要有形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} and {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} and is registered as a [[:en:Intangible cultural heritage|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage]]. The museum opened in [[:en:1968|1968]]{{Cite web|url=http://www.hidahachimangu.jp/hachiman/yuisho.html|title=Shrine History|publisher=Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|access-date=2022-03-21}}. It is located within the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine, which also manages the museum. The museum includes an annex, the {{ill|Sakurayama Nikko Hall|ja|桜山日光館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurayama Nikko Hall|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The museum is designated by [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]] as an equivalent to a museum (designated facility).{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/400187.pdf|title=Registered Museums and Designated Facilities in Gifu Prefecture|format=PDF|publisher=Gifu Prefecture|access-date=2024-06-30}} It is also registered under Gifu Prefecture's {{ill|Machikado Museums|ja|岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Machikado Museums|qq=}} program. == Main Exhibits == * Floats: Four out of the eleven floats from the Autumn Takayama Festival are displayed, with three changes annually. * Mikoshi (portable shrine) * Training Room * Treasures == Sakurayama Nikko Hall == * Opened in [[:en:1995|1995]]. * Displays a 1/10 scale model of the [[:en:Nikkō Tōshō-gū|Nikkō Tōshō-gū]], created over 15 years during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō period|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大正}} by a team of 33 artisans led by Hasegawa Kijūrō.The lead craftsman, originally from Toyama Prefecture. == Visitor Information == * Address: Sakura-machi 178, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture (on the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine) * Hours: 9:00 AM–5:00 PM (March–November), 9:00 On the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine | Latitude Degrees = 36 | Latitude Minutes = 8 | Latitude Seconds = 54.0 | N (North) or S (South) = N | Longitude Degrees = 137 | Longitude Minutes = 15 | Longitude Seconds = 36.5 | E (East) or W (West) = E | Map Country Code = JP-21 | Show Coordinates at Top = | Access = | URL = }} '''Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall''' (高山祭屋台会館, Takayama Matsuri Yatai Kaikan) is a [[:en:Museum|museum]] located in [[:en:Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]]. == Overview == The museum houses and exhibits the [[:en:Matsuri float|Floats]] from the {{ill|Takayama Festival|lt=Takayama Festival|lt=Autumn Takayama Festival|ja|高山祭#秋の八幡祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Takayama Festival|qq=}}, a festival celebrated as part of the {{ill|Sakurayama Hachimangu|lt=Sakurayama Hachimangu|lt=Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|ja|桜山八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|qq=}}'s {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Annual festivals|ja|例祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/annual festivals|qq=}}. The festival has been designated as a {{ill|National Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要有形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} and {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} and is registered as a [[:en:Intangible cultural heritage|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage]]. The museum opened in [[:en:1968|1968]]{{Cite web|url=http://www.hidahachimangu.jp/hachiman/yuisho.html|title=Shrine History|publisher=Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|access-date=2022-03-21}}. It is located within the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine, which also manages the museum. The museum includes an annex, the {{ill|Sakurayama Nikko Hall|ja|桜山日光館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurayama Nikko Hall|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The museum is designated by [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]] as an equivalent to a museum (designated facility).{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/400187.pdf|title=Registered Museums and Designated Facilities in Gifu Prefecture|format=PDF|publisher=Gifu Prefecture|access-date=2024-06-30}} It is also registered under Gifu Prefecture's {{ill|Machikado Museums|ja|岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Machikado Museums|qq=}} program. == Main Exhibits == * Floats: Four out of the eleven floats from the Autumn Takayama Festival are displayed, with three changes annually. * Mikoshi (portable shrine) * Training Room * Treasures == Sakurayama Nikko Hall == * Opened in [[:en:1995|1995]]. * Displays a 1/10 scale model of the [[:en:Nikkō Tōshō-gū|Nikkō Tōshō-gū]], created over 15 years during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō period|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大正|1=Taishō period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q329174}} by a team of 33 artisans led by Hasegawa Kijūrō.The lead craftsman, originally from Toyama Prefecture. == Visitor Information == * Address: Sakura-machi 178, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture (on the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine) * Hours: 9:00 AM–5:00 PM (March–November), 9:00 AM–4:30 PM (December–February){{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/256201.pdf|title=Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall & Sakurayama Nikko Hall|format=PDF|publisher=Gifu Prefecture|access-date=2022-03-21}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-gifu.jp/spot/detail_1244.html|title=Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine & Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall|publisher=Gifu Travel Guide|access-date=2022-03-21}} * Closed: Open year-round * Admission: Adults ¥1,000, High school students ¥600, Elementary/Junior high school students ¥500 (includes entry to both Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall and Sakurayama Nikko Hall) * Parking: 50 spaces (paid) == Access == === Public Transportation === * Approximately a 20-minute walk from [[:en:Takayama Station|Takayama Station]] on the [[:en:Takayama Main Line|JR Takayama Main Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:List of museums in Japan|List of museums in Japan]] * [[:en:Matsuri float|Floats]] * {{ill|Float Exhibition Halls|ja|山車会館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Float Exhibition Halls|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.hidahachimangu.jp/yataikaikan/index.html Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall] - Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine * [https://www.kankou-gifu.jp/spot/detail_1244.html Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine & Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall] - Gifu Travel Guide {{translated page|ja|高山祭屋台会館|version=102411490|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Folk museums in Japan]] [[Category:Museums in Takayama]] [[Category:Museums established in 1968]] }} [[ja:高山祭屋台会館]] [[Category:Museums in Takayama]] {{Commons category|Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall}} {{wikidata link|Q123476040}}

artisans led by Hasegawa Kijūrō.The lead craftsman, originally from Toyama Prefecture. == Visitor Information == * Address: Sakura-machi 178, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture (on the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine) * Hours: 9:00 AM–5:00 PM (March–November), 9:00 AM–4:30 PM (December–February){{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/256201.pdf|title=Takayama Festival Float school students ¥500 (includes entry to both Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall and Sakurayama Nikko Hall) * Parking: 50 spaces (paid) == Access == === Public Transportation === * Approximately a [[sn:commons:Category:Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall]][[ja:高山祭屋台会館]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q123476040]] Wikidata name: Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:45, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Museum | Name = Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall | image = [[File:Takayama festival floats exhibition hall, Takayama, 2015.jpg|300px]] | image Description = | Formal Name = | Nickname = | Predecessor = | Specialization = [[:en:Takayama Festival|Takayama Festival]] | Collection Size = | Visitors = | Director = | Curators = | Research Staff = | Operating Body = {{ill|Sakurayama Hachimangu|lt=Sakurayama Hachimangu|lt=Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|ja|桜山八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} | Management = | Annual Budget = | Building Design = | Site Area = | Building Area = | Total Floor Area = | Opened = [[:en:1968|1968]] | Closed = | Location Postal Code = 506-0858 | Location = [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]], [[:en:Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], Sakura-machi 178
On the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine | Latitude Degrees = 36 | Latitude Minutes = 8 | Latitude Seconds = 54.0 | N (North) or S (South) = N | Longitude Degrees = 137 | Longitude Minutes = 15 | Longitude Seconds = 36.5 | E (East) or W (West) = E | Map Country Code = JP-21 | Show Coordinates at Top = | Access = | URL = }} '''Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall''' (高山祭屋台会館, Takayama Matsuri Yatai Kaikan) is a [[:en:Museum|museum]] located in [[:en:Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]]. == Overview == The museum houses and exhibits the [[:en:Matsuri float|Floats]] from the {{ill|Takayama Festival|lt=Takayama Festival|lt=Autumn Takayama Festival|ja|高山祭#秋の八幡祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Takayama Festival|qq=}}, a festival celebrated as part of the {{ill|Sakurayama Hachimangu|lt=Sakurayama Hachimangu|lt=Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|ja|桜山八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|qq=}}'s {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Annual festivals|ja|例祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/annual festivals|qq=}}. The festival has been designated as a {{ill|National Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要有形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} and {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} and is registered as a [[:en:Intangible cultural heritage|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage]]. The museum opened in [[:en:1968|1968]]{{Cite web|url=http://www.hidahachimangu.jp/hachiman/yuisho.html|title=Shrine History|publisher=Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|access-date=2022-03-21}}. It is located within the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine, which also manages the museum. The museum includes an annex, the {{ill|Sakurayama Nikko Hall|ja|桜山日光館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurayama Nikko Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The museum is designated by [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]] as an equivalent to a museum (designated facility).{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/400187.pdf|title=Registered Museums and Designated Facilities in Gifu Prefecture|format=PDF|publisher=Gifu Prefecture|access-date=2024-06-30}} It is also registered under Gifu Prefecture's {{ill|Machikado Museums|ja|岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Machikado Museums|qq=}} program. == Main Exhibits == * Floats: Four out of the eleven floats from the Autumn Takayama Festival are displayed, with three changes annually. * Mikoshi (portable shrine) * Training Room * Treasures == Sakurayama Nikko Hall == * Opened in [[:en:1995|1995]]. * Displays a 1/10 scale model of the [[:en:Nikkō Tōshō-gū|Nikkō Tōshō-gū]], created over 15 years during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大正|1=Taishō period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q329174}} by a team of 33 The festival has been designated as a {{ill|National Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要有形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} and {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} and is registered as a [[:en:Intangible cultural heritage|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage]]. The museum opened in [[:en:1968|1968]]{{Cite web|url=http://www.hidahachimangu.jp/hachiman/yuisho.html|title=Shrine History|publisher=Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|access-date=2022-03-21}}. It is located within the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine, which also manages the museum. The museum includes an annex, the {{ill|Sakurayama Nikko Hall|ja|桜山日光館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurayama Nikko Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The museum is designated by [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]] as an equivalent to a museum (designated facility).{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/400187.pdf|title=Registered Museums and Designated Facilities in Gifu Prefecture|format=PDF|publisher=Gifu Prefecture|access-date=2024-06-30}} It is also registered under Gifu Prefecture's {{ill|Machikado Museums|ja|岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Machikado Museums|qq=}} program. == Main Exhibits == * Floats: Four out of the eleven floats from the Autumn Takayama Festival are displayed, with three changes annually. * Mikoshi (portable shrine) * Training Room * Treasures == Sakurayama Nikko Hall == * Opened in [[:en:1995|1995]]. * Displays a 1/10 scale model of the [[:en:Nikkō Tōshō-gū|Nikkō Tōshō-gū]], created over 15 years during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大正|1=Taishō period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q329174}} by a team of 33 artisans led by Hasegawa Kijūrō.The lead craftsman, originally from Toyama Prefecture. == Visitor Information == * Address: Sakura-machi 178, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture (on the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine) * Hours: 9:00 AM–5:00 PM (March–November), 9:00 AM–4:30 PM (December–February){{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/256201.pdf|title=Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall & Sakurayama Nikko Hall|format=PDF|publisher=Gifu Prefecture|access-date=2022-03-21}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-gifu.jp/spot/detail_1244.html|title=Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine & Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall|publisher=Gifu Travel Guide|access-date=2022-03-21}} * Closed: Open year-round * Admission: Adults ¥1,000, High school students ¥600, Elementary/Junior high school students ¥500 (includes entry to both Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall and Sakurayama Nikko Hall) * Parking: 50 spaces (paid) == Access == === Public Transportation === * Approximately a 20-minute walk from [[:en:Takayama Station|Takayama Station]] on the [[:en:Takayama Main Line|JR Takayama Main Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:List of museums in Japan|List of museums in Japan]] * [[:en:Matsuri float|Floats]] * {{ill|Float Exhibition Halls|ja|山車会館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Float Exhibition Halls|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.hidahachimangu.jp/yataikaikan/index.html Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall] - Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine * [https://www.kankou-gifu.jp/spot/detail_1244.html Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine & Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall] - Gifu Travel Guide {{translated page|ja|高山祭屋台会館|version=102411490|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Folk museums in Japan]] [[Category:Museums in Takayama]] [[Category:Museums established in 1968]] }} [[ja:高山祭屋台会館]] [[Category:Museums in Takayama]] {{Commons category|Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall}} {{wikidata link|Q123476040}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Guide|access-date=2022-03-21}} * Closed: Open year-round * Admission: Adults ¥1,000, High school students ¥600, Elementary/Junior high school students ¥500 (includes entry to both Takayama Festival [[sn:commons:Category:Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall]][[ja:高山祭屋台会館]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q123476040]] Wikidata name: Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:45, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Museum | Name = Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall | image = [[File:Takayama festival floats exhibition hall, Takayama, 2015.jpg|300px]] | image Description = | Formal Name = | Nickname = | Predecessor = | Specialization = [[:en:Takayama Festival|Takayama Festival]] | Collection Size = | Visitors = | Director = | Curators = | Research Staff = | Operating Body = {{ill|Sakurayama Hachimangu|lt=Sakurayama Hachimangu|lt=Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|ja|桜山八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} | Management = | Annual Budget = | Building Design = | Site Area = | Building Area = | Total Floor Area = | Opened = [[:en:1968|1968]] | Closed = | Location Postal Code = 506-0858 | Location = [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]], [[:en:Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], Sakura-machi 178
On the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine | Latitude Degrees = 36 | Latitude Minutes = 8 | Latitude Seconds = 54.0 | N (North) or S (South) = N | Longitude Degrees = 137 | Longitude Minutes = 15 | Longitude Seconds = 36.5 | E (East) or W (West) = E | Map Country Code = JP-21 | Show Coordinates at Top = | Access = | URL = }} '''Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall''' (高山祭屋台会館, Takayama Matsuri Yatai Kaikan) is a [[:en:Museum|museum]] located in [[:en:Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]]. == Overview == The museum houses and exhibits the [[:en:Matsuri float|Floats]] from the {{ill|Takayama Festival|lt=Takayama Festival|lt=Autumn Takayama Festival|ja|高山祭#秋の八幡祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Takayama Festival|qq=}}, a festival celebrated as part of the {{ill|Sakurayama Hachimangu|lt=Sakurayama Hachimangu|lt=Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|ja|桜山八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|qq=}}'s {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Annual festivals|ja|例祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/annual festivals|qq=}}. The festival has been designated as a {{ill|National Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要有形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} and {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} and is registered as a [[:en:Intangible cultural heritage|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage]]. The museum opened in [[:en:1968|1968]]{{Cite web|url=http://www.hidahachimangu.jp/hachiman/yuisho.html|title=Shrine History|publisher=Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|access-date=2022-03-21}}. It is located within the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine, which also manages the museum. The museum includes an annex, the {{ill|Sakurayama Nikko Hall|ja|桜山日光館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurayama Nikko Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The museum is designated by [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]] as an equivalent to a museum (designated facility).{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/400187.pdf|title=Registered Museums and Designated Facilities in Gifu Prefecture|format=PDF|publisher=Gifu Prefecture|access-date=2024-06-30}} It is also registered under Gifu Prefecture's {{ill|Machikado Museums|ja|岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Machikado Museums|qq=}} program. == Main Exhibits == * Floats: Four out of the eleven floats from the Autumn Takayama Festival are displayed, with three changes annually. * Mikoshi (portable shrine) * Training Room * Treasures == Sakurayama Nikko Hall == * Opened in [[:en:1995|1995]]. * Displays a 1/10 scale model of the [[:en:Nikkō Tōshō-gū|Nikkō Tōshō-gū]], created over 15 years during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大正|1=Taishō period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q329174}} by a team of 33 artisans led by Hasegawa Kijūrō.The lead craftsman, originally from Toyama Prefecture. == Visitor Information == * Address: Sakura-machi 178, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture (on the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine) * Hours: 9:00 AM–5:00 PM (March–November), 9:00 AM–4:30 PM The festival has been designated as a {{ill|National Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要有形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} and {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} and is registered as a [[:en:Intangible cultural heritage|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage]]. The museum opened in [[:en:1968|1968]]{{Cite web|url=http://www.hidahachimangu.jp/hachiman/yuisho.html|title=Shrine History|publisher=Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|access-date=2022-03-21}}. It is located within the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine, which also manages the museum. The museum includes an annex, the {{ill|Sakurayama Nikko Hall|ja|桜山日光館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurayama Nikko Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The museum is designated by [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]] as an equivalent to a museum (designated facility).{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/400187.pdf|title=Registered Museums and Designated Facilities in Gifu Prefecture|format=PDF|publisher=Gifu Prefecture|access-date=2024-06-30}} It is also registered under Gifu Prefecture's {{ill|Machikado Museums|ja|岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Machikado Museums|qq=}} program. == Main Exhibits == * Floats: Four out of the eleven floats from the Autumn Takayama Festival are displayed, with three changes annually. * Mikoshi (portable shrine) * Training Room * Treasures == Sakurayama Nikko Hall == * Opened in [[:en:1995|1995]]. * Displays a 1/10 scale model of the [[:en:Nikkō Tōshō-gū|Nikkō Tōshō-gū]], created over 15 years during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大正|1=Taishō period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q329174}} by a team of 33 artisans led by Hasegawa Kijūrō.The lead craftsman, originally from Toyama Prefecture. == Visitor Information == * Address: Sakura-machi 178, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture (on the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine) * Hours: 9:00 AM–5:00 PM (March–November), 9:00 AM–4:30 PM (December–February){{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/256201.pdf|title=Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall & Sakurayama Nikko Hall|format=PDF|publisher=Gifu Prefecture|access-date=2022-03-21}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-gifu.jp/spot/detail_1244.html|title=Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine & Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall|publisher=Gifu Travel Guide|access-date=2022-03-21}} * Closed: Open year-round * Admission: Adults ¥1,000, High school students ¥600, Elementary/Junior high school students ¥500 (includes entry to both Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall and Sakurayama Nikko Hall) * Parking: 50 spaces (paid) == Access == === Public Transportation === * Approximately a 20-minute walk from [[:en:Takayama Station|Takayama Station]] on the [[:en:Takayama Main Line|JR Takayama Main Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:List of museums in Japan|List of museums in Japan]] * [[:en:Matsuri float|Floats]] * {{ill|Float Exhibition Halls|ja|山車会館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Float Exhibition Halls|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.hidahachimangu.jp/yataikaikan/index.html Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall] - Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine * [https://www.kankou-gifu.jp/spot/detail_1244.html Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine & Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall] - Gifu Travel Guide {{translated page|ja|高山祭屋台会館|version=102411490|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Folk museums in Japan]] [[Category:Museums in Takayama]] On the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine | Latitude Degrees = 36 | Latitude Minutes = 8 | Latitude Seconds = 54.0 | N (North) or S (South) = N | Longitude Degrees = 137 | Longitude Minutes = 15 | Longitude Seconds = 36.5 | E (East) or W (West) = E | Map Country Code = JP-21 | Show Coordinates at Top = | Access = | URL = }} '''Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall''' (高山祭屋台会館, Takayama Matsuri Yatai Kaikan) is a [[:en:Museum|museum]] located in [[:en:Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]]. == Overview == The museum houses and exhibits the [[:en:Matsuri float|Floats]] from the {{ill|Takayama Festival|lt=Takayama Festival|lt=Autumn Takayama Festival|ja|高山祭#秋の八幡祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Takayama Festival|qq=}}, a festival celebrated as part of the {{ill|Sakurayama Hachimangu|lt=Sakurayama Hachimangu|lt=Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|ja|桜山八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|qq=}}'s {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Annual festivals|ja|例祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/annual festivals|qq=}}. The festival has been designated as a {{ill|National Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要有形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} and {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} and is registered as a [[:en:Intangible cultural heritage|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage]]. The museum opened in [[:en:1968|1968]]{{Cite web|url=http://www.hidahachimangu.jp/hachiman/yuisho.html|title=Shrine History|publisher=Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|access-date=2022-03-21}}. It is located within the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine, which also manages the museum. The museum includes an annex, the {{ill|Sakurayama Nikko Hall|ja|桜山日光館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurayama Nikko Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The museum is designated by [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]] as an equivalent to a museum (designated facility).{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/400187.pdf|title=Registered Museums and Designated Facilities in Gifu Prefecture|format=PDF|publisher=Gifu Prefecture|access-date=2024-06-30}} It is also registered under Gifu Prefecture's {{ill|Machikado Museums|ja|岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Machikado Museums|qq=}} program. == Main Exhibits == * Floats: Four out of the eleven floats from the Autumn Takayama Festival are displayed, with three changes annually. * Mikoshi (portable shrine) * Training Room * Treasures == Sakurayama Nikko Hall == * Opened in [[:en:1995|1995]]. * Displays a 1/10 scale model of the [[:en:Nikkō Tōshō-gū|Nikkō Tōshō-gū]], created over 15 years during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大正|1=Taishō period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q329174}} by a team of 33 artisans led by Hasegawa Kijūrō.The lead craftsman, originally from Toyama On the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine | Latitude Degrees = 36 | Latitude Minutes = 8 | Latitude Seconds = 54.0 | N (North) or S (South) = N | Longitude Degrees = 137 | Longitude Minutes = 15 | Longitude Seconds = 36.5 | E (East) or W (West) = E | Map Country Code = JP-21 | Show Coordinates at Top = | Access = | URL = }} '''Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall''' (高山祭屋台会館, Takayama Matsuri Yatai Kaikan) is a {{ill|Museum|en|Museum|WD=Q33506|lt=museum}} located in {{ill|Takayama, Gifu|en|Takayama, Gifu|WD=Q622594|lt=Takayama}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}}. == Overview == The museum houses and exhibits the {{ill|Matsuri float|en|Matsuri float|WD=Q11470974|lt=Floats}} from the {{ill|Takayama Festival|lt=Takayama Festival|lt=Autumn Takayama Festival|ja|高山祭#秋の八幡祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Takayama Festival|qq=}}, a festival celebrated as part of the {{ill|Sakurayama Hachimangu|lt=Sakurayama Hachimangu|lt=Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|ja|桜山八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|qq=}}'s {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Annual festivals|ja|例祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/annual festivals|qq=}}. The festival has been designated as a {{ill|National Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要有形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} and {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} and is registered as a {{ill|Intangible cultural heritage|en|Intangible cultural heritage|WD=Q84036549|lt=UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage}}. The museum opened in {{ill|1968|en|1968|WD=Q2644|lt=1968}}{{Cite web|url=http://www.hidahachimangu.jp/hachiman/yuisho.html|title=Shrine History|publisher=Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|access-date=2022-03-21}}. It is located within the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine, which also manages the museum. The museum includes an annex, the {{ill|Sakurayama Nikko Hall|ja|桜山日光館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurayama Nikko Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The museum is designated by {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}} as an equivalent to a museum (designated facility).{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/400187.pdf|title=Registered Museums and Designated Facilities in Gifu Prefecture|format=PDF|publisher=Gifu Prefecture|access-date=2024-06-30}} It is also registered under Gifu Prefecture's {{ill|Machikado Museums|ja|岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Machikado Museums|qq=}} program. == Main Exhibits == * Floats: Four out of the eleven floats from the Autumn Takayama Festival are displayed, with three changes annually. * Mikoshi (portable shrine) * Training Room * Treasures == Sakurayama Nikko Hall == * Opened in {{ill|1995|en|1995|WD=Q2068|lt=1995}}. * Displays a 1/10 scale model of the {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|en|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|WD=Q696641|lt=Nikkō Tōshō-gū}}, created over 15 years during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大正|1=Taishō period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q329174}} by a team of 33 artisans led by Hasegawa Kijūrō.The lead craftsman, originally from Toyama Prefecture. == Visitor Information == * Address: Sakura-machi 178, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture (on the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine) * Hours: 9:00 AM–5:00 PM (March–November), 9:00 AM–4:30 PM (December–February){{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/256201.pdf|title=Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall & Sakurayama Nikko Hall|format=PDF|publisher=Gifu Prefecture|access-date=2022-03-21}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-gifu.jp/spot/detail_1244.html|title=Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine & Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall|publisher=Gifu Travel Guide|access-date=2022-03-21}} * Closed: Open year-round * Admission: Adults ¥1,000, High school students ¥600, Elementary/Junior high school students ¥500 (includes entry to both Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall and Sakurayama Nikko Hall) * Parking: 50 spaces (paid) == Access == === Public Transportation === * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Takayama Station|en|Takayama Station|WD=Q874020|lt=Takayama Station}} on the {{ill|Takayama Main Line|en|Takayama Main Line|WD=Q1188357|lt=JR Takayama Main Line}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of museums in Japan|en|List of museums in Japan|WD=Q3252554|lt=List of museums in Japan}} * {{ill|Matsuri float|en|Matsuri float|WD=Q11470974|lt=Floats}} * {{ill|Float Exhibition Halls|ja|山車会館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Float Exhibition Halls|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.hidahachimangu.jp/yataikaikan/index.html Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall] - Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine * [https://www.kankou-gifu.jp/spot/detail_1244.html Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine & Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall] - Gifu Travel Guide {{translated page|ja|高山祭屋台会館|version=102411490|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:高山祭屋台会館]] {{Commons category|Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall}} {{wikidata link|Q123476040}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Folk museums in Japan]] [[Category:Museums established in 1968]] [[Category:Museums in Takayama]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

On the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine | Latitude Degrees = 36 | Latitude Minutes = 8 | Latitude Seconds = 54.0 | N (North) or S (South) = N | Longitude Degrees = 137 | Longitude Minutes = 15 | Longitude Seconds = 36.5 | E (East) or W (West) = E | Map Country Code = JP-21 | Show Coordinates at Top = | Access = | URL = }} '''Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall''' (高山祭屋台会館, Takayama Matsuri Yatai Kaikan) is a {{ill|Museum|en|Museum|WD=Q33506|lt=museum}} located in {{ill|Takayama, Gifu|en|Takayama, Gifu|WD=Q622594|lt=Takayama}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}}. == Overview == The museum houses and exhibits the {{ill|Matsuri float|en|Matsuri float|WD=Q11470974|lt=Floats}} from the {{ill|Takayama Festival|lt=Takayama Festival|lt=Autumn Takayama Festival|ja|高山祭#秋の八幡祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Takayama Festival|qq=}}, a festival celebrated as part of the {{ill|Sakurayama Hachimangu|lt=Sakurayama Hachimangu|lt=Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|ja|桜山八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|qq=}}'s {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Annual festivals|ja|例祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/annual festivals|qq=}}. The festival has been designated as a {{ill|National Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要有形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} and {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} and is registered as a {{ill|Intangible cultural heritage|en|Intangible cultural heritage|WD=Q84036549|lt=UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage}}. The museum opened in {{ill|1968|en|1968|WD=Q2644|lt=1968}}{{Cite web|url=http://www.hidahachimangu.jp/hachiman/yuisho.html|title=Shrine History|publisher=Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|access-date=2022-03-21}}. It is located within the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine, which also manages the museum. The museum includes an annex, the {{ill|Sakurayama Nikko Hall|ja|桜山日光館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurayama Nikko Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The museum is designated by {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}} as an equivalent to a museum (designated facility).{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/400187.pdf|title=Registered Museums and Designated Facilities in Gifu Prefecture|format=PDF|publisher=Gifu Prefecture|access-date=2024-06-30}} It is also registered under Gifu Prefecture's {{ill|Machikado Museums|ja|岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Machikado Museums|qq=}} program. == Main Exhibits == * Floats: Four out of the eleven floats from the Autumn Takayama Festival are displayed, with three changes annually. * Mikoshi (portable shrine) * Training Room * Treasures == Sakurayama Nikko Hall == * Opened in {{ill|1995|en|1995|WD=Q2068|lt=1995}}. * Displays a 1/10 scale model of the {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|en|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|WD=Q696641|lt=Nikkō Tōshō-gū}}, created over 15 years during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大正|1=Taishō period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q329174}} by a team of 33 artisans led by Hasegawa Kijūrō.The lead craftsman, originally from Toyama Prefecture. == Visitor Information == * Address: Sakura-machi 178, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture (on the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine) * Hours: 9:00 AM–5:00 PM (March–November), 9:00 AM–4:30 PM (December–February){{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/256201.pdf|title=Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall & Sakurayama Nikko Hall|format=PDF|publisher=Gifu Prefecture|access-date=2022-03-21}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-gifu.jp/spot/detail_1244.html|title=Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine & Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall|publisher=Gifu Travel Guide|access-date=2022-03-21}} * Closed: Open year-round * Admission: Adults ¥1,000, High school students ¥600, Elementary/Junior high school students ¥500 (includes entry to both Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall and Sakurayama Nikko Hall) * Parking: 50 spaces (paid) == Access == === Public Transportation === * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Takayama Station|en|Takayama Station|WD=Q874020|lt=Takayama Station}} on the {{ill|Takayama Main Line|en|Takayama Main Line|WD=Q1188357|lt=JR Takayama Main Line}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of museums in Japan|en|List of museums in Japan|WD=Q3252554|lt=List of museums in Japan}} * {{ill|Matsuri float|en|Matsuri float|WD=Q11470974|lt=Floats}} * {{ill|Float Exhibition Halls|ja|山車会館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Float Exhibition Halls|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.hidahachimangu.jp/yataikaikan/index.html Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall] - Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine * [https://www.kankou-gifu.jp/spot/detail_1244.html Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine & Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall] - Gifu Travel Guide {{translated page|ja|高山祭屋台会館|version=102411490|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:高山祭屋台会館]] {{Commons category|Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall}} {{wikidata link|Q123476040}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Folk museums in Japan]] [[Category:Museums established in 1968]] [[Category:Museums in Takayama]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

On the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine | Latitude Degrees = 36 | Latitude Minutes = 8 | Latitude Seconds = 54.0 | N (North) or S (South) = N | Longitude Degrees = 137 | Longitude Minutes = 15 | Longitude Seconds = 36.5 | E (East) or W (West) = E | Map Country Code = JP-21 | Show Coordinates at Top = | Access = | URL = }} '''Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall''' (高山祭屋台会館, Takayama Matsuri Yatai Kaikan) is a {{ill|Museum|en|Museum|WD=Q33506|lt=museum|1=Museum}} located in {{ill|Takayama, Gifu|en|Takayama, Gifu|WD=Q622594|lt=Takayama|1=Takayama, Gifu}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. == Overview == The museum houses and exhibits the {{ill|Matsuri float|en|Matsuri float|WD=Q11470974|lt=Floats|1=Matsuri float}} from the {{ill|Takayama Festival|lt=Takayama Festival|lt=Autumn Takayama Festival|ja|高山祭#秋の八幡祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Takayama Festival|qq=}}, a festival celebrated as part of the {{ill|Sakurayama Hachimangu|lt=Sakurayama Hachimangu|lt=Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|ja|桜山八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|qq=}}'s {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Annual festivals|ja|例祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/annual festivals|qq=}}. The festival has been designated as a {{ill|National Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要有形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} and {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} and is registered as a {{ill|Intangible cultural heritage|en|Intangible cultural heritage|WD=Q84036549|lt=UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|1=Intangible cultural heritage}}. The museum opened in {{ill|1968|en|1968|WD=Q2644|lt=1968|1=1968}}{{Cite web|url=http://www.hidahachimangu.jp/hachiman/yuisho.html|title=Shrine History|publisher=Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|access-date=2022-03-21}}. It is located within the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine, which also manages the museum. The museum includes an annex, the {{ill|Sakurayama Nikko Hall|ja|桜山日光館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurayama Nikko Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The museum is designated by {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}} as an equivalent to a museum (designated facility).{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/400187.pdf|title=Registered Museums and Designated Facilities in Gifu Prefecture|format=PDF|publisher=Gifu Prefecture|access-date=2024-06-30}} It is also registered under Gifu Prefecture's {{ill|Machikado Museums|ja|岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Machikado Museums|qq=}} program. == Main Exhibits == * Floats: Four out of the eleven floats from the Autumn Takayama Festival are displayed, with three changes annually. * Mikoshi (portable shrine) * Training Room * Treasures == Sakurayama Nikko Hall == * Opened in {{ill|1995|en|1995|WD=Q2068|lt=1995|1=1995}}. * Displays a 1/10 scale model of the {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|en|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|WD=Q696641|lt=Nikkō Tōshō-gū|1=Nikkō Tōshō-gū}}, created over 15 years during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大正|1=Taishō era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q329174}} by a team of 33 artisans led by Hasegawa Kijūrō.The lead craftsman, originally from Toyama Prefecture. == Visitor Information == * Address: Sakura-machi 178, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture (on the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine) * Hours: 9:00 AM–5:00 PM (March–November), 9:00 AM–4:30 PM (December–February){{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/256201.pdf|title=Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall & Sakurayama Nikko Hall|format=PDF|publisher=Gifu Prefecture|access-date=2022-03-21}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-gifu.jp/spot/detail_1244.html|title=Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine & Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall|publisher=Gifu Travel Guide|access-date=2022-03-21}} * Closed: Open year-round * Admission: Adults ¥1,000, High school students ¥600, Elementary/Junior high school students ¥500 (includes entry to both Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall and Sakurayama Nikko Hall) * Parking: 50 spaces (paid) == Access == === Public Transportation === * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Takayama Station|en|Takayama Station|WD=Q874020|lt=Takayama Station|1=Takayama Station}} on the {{ill|Takayama Main Line|en|Takayama Main Line|WD=Q1188357|lt=JR Takayama Main Line|1=Takayama Main Line}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of museums in Japan|en|List of museums in Japan|WD=Q3252554|lt=List of museums in Japan|1=List of museums in Japan}} * {{ill|Matsuri float|en|Matsuri float|WD=Q11470974|lt=Floats|1=Matsuri float}} * {{ill|Float Exhibition Halls|ja|山車会館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Float Exhibition Halls|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.hidahachimangu.jp/yataikaikan/index.html Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall] - Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine * [https://www.kankou-gifu.jp/spot/detail_1244.html Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine & Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall] - Gifu Travel Guide {{translated page|ja|高山祭屋台会館|version=102411490|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:高山祭屋台会館]] {{Commons category|Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall}} {{wikidata link|Q123476040}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Folk museums in Japan]] [[Category:Museums established in 1968]] [[Category:Museums in Takayama]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

On the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine | Latitude Degrees = 36 | Latitude Minutes = 8 | Latitude Seconds = 54.0 | N (North) or S (South) = N | Longitude Degrees = 137 | Longitude Minutes = 15 | Longitude Seconds = 36.5 | E (East) or W (West) = E | Map Country Code = JP-21 | Show Coordinates at Top = | Access = | URL = }} '''Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall''' (高山祭屋台会館, Takayama Matsuri Yatai Kaikan) is a {{ill|Museum|en|Museum|WD=Q33506|lt=museum|1=Museum}} located in {{ill|Takayama, Gifu|en|Takayama, Gifu|WD=Q622594|lt=Takayama|1=Takayama, Gifu}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. == Overview == The museum houses and exhibits the {{ill|Matsuri float|en|Matsuri float|WD=Q11470974|lt=Floats|1=Matsuri float}} from the {{ill|Takayama Festival|lt=Takayama Festival|lt=Autumn Takayama Festival|ja|高山祭#秋の八幡祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Takayama Festival|qq=}}, a festival celebrated as part of the {{ill|Sakurayama Hachimangu|lt=Sakurayama Hachimangu|lt=Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|ja|桜山八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|qq=}}'s {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Annual festivals|ja|例祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/annual festivals|qq=}}. The festival has been designated as a {{ill|National Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要有形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} and {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} and is registered as a {{ill|Intangible cultural heritage|en|Intangible cultural heritage|WD=Q84036549|lt=UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|1=Intangible cultural heritage}}. The museum opened in {{ill|1968|en|1968|WD=Q2644|lt=1968|1=1968}}{{Cite web|url=http://www.hidahachimangu.jp/hachiman/yuisho.html|title=Shrine History|publisher=Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine|access-date=2022-03-21}}. It is located within the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine, which also manages the museum. The museum includes an annex, the {{ill|Sakurayama Nikko Hall|ja|桜山日光館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurayama Nikko Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The museum is designated by {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}} as an equivalent to a museum (designated facility).{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/400187.pdf|title=Registered Museums and Designated Facilities in Gifu Prefecture|format=PDF|publisher=Gifu Prefecture|access-date=2024-06-30}} It is also registered under Gifu Prefecture's {{ill|Machikado Museums|ja|岐阜県まちかど美術館・博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Machikado Museums|qq=}} program. == Main Exhibits == * Floats: Four out of the eleven floats from the Autumn Takayama Festival are displayed, with three changes annually. * Mikoshi (portable shrine) * Training Room * Treasures == Sakurayama Nikko Hall == * Opened in {{ill|1995|en|1995|WD=Q2068|lt=1995|1=1995}}. * Displays a 1/10 scale model of the {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|en|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|WD=Q696641|lt=Nikkō Tōshō-gū|1=Nikkō Tōshō-gū}}, created over 15 years during the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大正|1=Taishō era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q329174}} by a team of 33 artisans led by Hasegawa Kijūrō.The lead craftsman, originally from Toyama Prefecture. == Visitor Information == * Address: Sakura-machi 178, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture (on the grounds of Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine) * Hours: 9:00 AM–5:00 PM (March–November), 9:00 AM–4:30 PM (December–February){{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/256201.pdf|title=Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall & Sakurayama Nikko Hall|format=PDF|publisher=Gifu Prefecture|access-date=2022-03-21}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-gifu.jp/spot/detail_1244.html|title=Sakurayama Hachiman Shrine & Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall|publisher=Gifu Travel Guide|access-date=2022-03-21}} * Closed: Open year-round * Admission: Adults ¥1,000, High school students ¥600, Elementary/Junior high school students ¥500 (includes entry to both Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall and Sakurayama Nikko Hall) * Parking: 50 spaces (paid) == Access == === Public Transportation === * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Takayama Station|en|Takayama Station|WD=Q874020|lt=Takayama Station|1=Takayama Station}} on the {{ill|Takayama Main Line|en|Takayama Main Line|WD=Q1188357|lt=JR Takayama Main Line|1=Takayama Main Line}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of museums in Japan|en|List of museums in Japan|WD=Q3252554|lt=List of museums in Japan|1=List of museums in Japan}} * {{ill|Matsuri float|en|Matsuri float|WD=Q11470974|lt=Floats|1=Matsuri float}} * {{ill|Float Exhibition Halls|ja|山車会館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Float Exhibition Halls|qq=}} == External
